# MHWilds: Endemic Life List

MHWilds: Weapons ・ MHWilds: Armors ・ MHWilds: Monsters ・ MHWilds: Monster Material List ・ MHWilds: Palico Gear ・ MHWilds: Locations ・ MHWilds: Skills ・ MHWilds: Item List ・ MHWilds: Assigned Quests ・ MHWilds: Optional Quests ・ MHWilds: Event Quests ・ MHWilds: Endemic Life List ・ MHWilds: Decorations ・ MHWilds: Charm List Contents 1 Endemic Life 2 Aquatic Life 3 Gallery 4 Trivia Endemic Life [ ] Icon Name Description Location Vigorwasp Insects that gather restorative nectar from Vitalilies and store it in their abdomens. While the flowers and nectar have similar properties, the latter can be scattered in large quantities to produce a lingering, continual healing effect. There's a rise in the Vigorwasp population during the Plenty, when Vitalilies flourish. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Ruins of Wyveria Giant Vigorwasp Large wasps that carry more nectar at a higher concentration. This boosts the range and potency of the nectar's restorative effects. They are most prevalent during the Plenty, when the Vigorwasps have learned the nectar will be more plentiful. They are sometimes sighted in groups. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Ruins of Wyveria Purewasp Insects that store nectar in their abdomens with restorative, detoxifying, and deodorizing properties. Since the effects are different from a Vigorwasp's, it is believed that this species collects ingredients from medicinal plants other than Vitalilies. However, based on ongoing research, it has been found that enzymes in their bodies could also play a role. Oilwell Basin Ruins of Wyveria Vigormantle Bug Insects that wear healing herbs like cloaks, initiating a fermentation process that creates a nectar with similar restorative properties. Collecting the cloak by hand or with the Hook Slinger will restore health. The cloak stores nutrients but can be shed in a pinch as a distraction to defend against predators. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Oilwell Basin Ruins of Wyveria Chillmantle Bug Insects that live in extremely hot environments. They wrap themselves in a case of plant matter—much like their mantle bug brethren—but the plants they use have cooling properties, allowing them to withstand heat. They are able to survive without the casing by burrowing underground. Oilwell Basin Heatmantle Bug Insects that live in extremely cold environments. They wrap themselves in plant matter-much like their mantle bug brethren-but they use plants with heating properties, such as hot peppers, to mitigate the cold. They can live on the surface thanks to this, but if uncloaked they must burrow underground to survive. Iceshard Cliffs Wiggly Litchi Insects that thrive in rotting wood and soil due to their diet of plants and rotting leaves. Consuming them will increase stamina and halve its depletion rate. Their high nutritional value makes them a common snack for some monsters. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Oilwell Basin Ruins of Wyveria Paratoad Toads containing a paralytic gas that are usually found by the water. They are sensitive to force and will release the gas as a defensive measure; it's powerful enough to stop hunters and large monsters in their tracks, which buys the Paratoad time to escape. Hunters can use this behavior to direct the gas at a desired target and gain the upper hand. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Ruins of Wyveria Nitrotoad Toads that store an explosive liquid within their bodies. Any physical stimulus causes them to spray this liquid and flee. Once the liquid contacts the air, it detonates, wreaking havoc. Though these creatures prefer to live in environments abounding in combustible materials like coal, their diet actually consists mostly of insects and crustaceans. It is yet unknown what causes them to produce the explosive liquid. Oilwell Basin Ruins of Wyveria Sleeptoad Toads that store sleeping gas in their bodies. A shock will cause them to spray the gas, putting the enemy to sleep while they escape. The gas is so potent that it affects hunters and even large monsters. You may be able to gain the upper hand if you use this to your advantage and direct the gas at a monster. Oilwell Basin Ruins of Wyveria Flashflies Insects that flash when struck. Usually, they live solitary existences with too faint a flicker to even notice. In caves or the dark of night, they form colonies where their light is more intense; striking them causes them to shine even brighter. On hunts, they can be used like flash bombs to blind and disorient monsters. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Oilwell Basin Ruins of Wyveria Dung Beetle Insects that habitually roll up monster dung. They bring it back to their underground nests not just for food, but to lay eggs on and feed their larvae. They're the faces of feces across the land: nature's diligent decomposers. The compacted dung pods formed by their efforts can be used as Slinger ammo. It's foul enough to send even large monsters running for fresh air—a useful trick when you're outnumbered. Windward Plains Oilwell Basin Bomb Beetle Beetles that roll around explosive rocks. Because both these rocks and their habitat are extremely hot, their bodies have evolved to withstand heat. It's yet unclear what the do with the rocks once they get back to their nests, but some theorize that they are fed to the beetles' young. Oilwell Basin Rime Beetle Beetles that gather up snow. Their snowballs can be used as Frostburst Ammo. Due to their ecology, Rime Beetles are limited to cold habitats and are more tolerant of cold than other insects. No one knows why they roll&#160;their snowballs back to their nests. Some theorize it's to grow the organic matter they feed on, while others think it could be related to reproductive behavior. Iceshard Cliffs Thunderbug Insects capable of discharging small amounts of electricity. They are drawn to electrical sources, particularly the fulgurite formed by lightning strikes. Thunderbug materials can be used to craft many useful tools such as shock traps and Thunder Ammo. Windward Plains Ruins of Wyveria Great Thunderbugs A large colony of Thunderbugs that reproduced rapidly as a result of the sandstorm lightning. A population this dense can produce enough current to stop even large monsters in their tracks. Since Thunderbugs are the fuel behind shock traps, there's merit to trying to lead large monsters into the colony as a way to gain the upper hand. Windward Plains Ruins of Wyveria Flashbug Insects that emit strong bursts of light in response to external factors. It was long believed that blunt trauma or death were the triggers, but this theory has been rejected. It is now thought that some constituent element of their body fluid vaporizes and produces the flash. In any case, their materials are highly prized for their utility in items like Slinger flash pods. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Oilwell Basin Ruins of Wyveria Godbug Insects that are claimed by some tomes and oral histories to live for a thousand years. No such specimens have been discovered, but studies of the Godbug's tissue prove that they do enjoy significant longevity. Their body fluids have diffusive properties that can be combined with other items to create powders with the same attributes. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Oilwell Basin Ruins of Wyveria Bitterbug Insects whose bitter extracts are used for a wide variety of medicinal purposes. In addition to promoting natural healing, disinfecting, and mitigating poisons, they also boost the efficacy of other medicines, making them highly useful. That said, by themselves they have little effect and taste extremely bitter, so it's best to consider them an ingredient, not a solution in their own right. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Oilwell Basin Ruins of Wyveria Dark Hornet Nocturnal hornets with strong defensive instincts. When they sense danger, they use a luminous organ to illuminate the area to check for threats, if one is spotted, they attack relentlessly with their poisonous stingers. Unlike other endemic life, Dark Hornets will readily attack large monsters that disturb their nests. Their diet also doesn't stimulate honey production, so hunters are better off steering clear. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Ruins of Wyveria Wedge Beetle Insects with robust shells that can withstand most lesser impacts. They cling firmly to walls and other surfaces, making them excellent latching points for the Slinger, whether it's to get around or simply to hang out indefinitely. However, a Wedge Beetle in flight can only bear the weight for a limited time, so it's best to latch on briefly to gain some height. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Oilwell Basin Ruins of Wyveria Windrustler Plains dwellers that move in packs. They use their arrow-like crests to coordinate movements and alert each other to threats. Since they stand their crests up when people and monsters draw near, that gesture is believed to indicate danger. Windrustlers have been sighted in Kunafa, a sign that they have learned two things: people aren't always a threat...and where there's people, there's food. Windward Plains Black Windrustler A rare variant occasionally spotted in a Windrustler pack, with charcoal skin and a larger frame. While it could be a type of melanistic mutation, there are enough unique examples to suggest this might even be a subspecies. Their role in the pack and relationship with others of their kind are important topics that warrant further study. Windward Plains Nothpecker Birds that live in symbiosis with the Ceratonoths and are often seen perched on their backs. They use their plier-like beaks to pick away old scales and feed on insects that have wormed their way into the cracks. In return for this grooming service, the Ceratonoths give the Nothpeckers a place to feed and shelter from outside threats- a win-win situation for all creatures involved. Windward Plains Landlight Shrimp Shrimp-like creatures that use their secretions to comb through fulgurite and feed on organic matter like dead Thunderbugs. The consumption of some of these microbes seems to have given the Landlight Shrimp a kind of luminescence. They use hook-like appendages to cling to rock faces in large numbers, happily eating away at the fulgurite and occasionally triggering falling boulders. Windward Plains Amstrigian Migrant birds that live in the forest, but travel to the plains during the Plenty to raise their young. Amstrigians choose lifelong mates and share the work when it comes to gathering food, building nests, and minding their young. Their meal of choice is insects, particularly Emperor Hoppers. They use their syrinxes to sing in chorus at the turning of the season, a social ritual that doubles as a form of communication. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Hymstrigian Rare birds with beautiful voices. Though similar to the Amstrigian, their stunning songs and colorful feathers distinguish them. Not much is known about them due to their rarity. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Rufflizard Lizards with adipose tissue near their jaws and tail. When food is plentiful, these sac-like reservoirs fatten up and turn a healthy red. A Rufflizard with plenty of food in the tank is a happy Rufflizard, and you may see them basking in the sun and bobbing to each other in greeting. A poorly fed Rufflizard however, will move as little as possible. Windward Plains Tracktail Lizard Endemic creatures that are native to the Windward Plains-particularly its deserts. Thanks to their unusual tails, they can sit upright and assess their surroundings, or they can shield themselves from the hot sun. When they sit, their tails leave a mark in the sand that resembles a large monster's footprint. They typically travel in pairs to watch each other's back while doing things like drinking. Windward Plains Pallbug Insects that feed on dead flesh and serve as decomposers in the ecosystem. They use the bristles on their forelegs and joints to sweep organic matter from the carrion. When they're not feeding on dead monsters, they can usually be found lurking in between rocks or other places the sun doesn't reach. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Oilwell Basin Ruins of Wyveria Quatreflies Flying insects that resemble four-leaf clovers. Resilient to any weather, they can be found day or night in nearly any climate at places where monsters aren't likely to prowl. Which is a good indicator that an area is safe enough for a Pop-up camp. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Oilwell Basin Ruins of Wyveria Emperor Hopper Huge grasshoppers that have fully earned their name. Their powerful hind legs allow them to jump long distances and escape in a pinch. If you rustle through the grass-their usual hiding spot-you're bound to see at least one Emperor Hopper spring away. Grass fires throw them into a panic, a situation that certain other hungry creatures might be eager to take advantage of. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Tyrant Hopper A phase mutation of the Emperor Hopper. Their bodies are reddish-black and are more ferocious. While Hoppers are usually herbivorous, this type feeds on carrion. Due to similarities across different regions, it&#160;is believed the mutation must occur in environments where plants are scarce. This causal relationship between a creature's formative environment and its mutations has become a major subject of research. Ruins of Wyveria Sandstar Nocturnal desert creatures with pale, luminescent fur that is believed to be the result of the insects they primarily feed on. Highly vigilant, they use their excellent hearing to detect approaching threats and flee, so trying to chase one down is the wrong approach. You'll have a far easier time catching one if you use a sonic bomb to disrupt its hearing. Windward Plains Leaflugger Ant Forest ants known for forming ranks and carrying leaves. Their sharp mandibles consist of a sharp edge for cutting and a separate apparatus for bending the leaves back behind their heads for easy carrying. Leaflugger Ants use these leaves as substrates to grow fungi, their primary sustenance. As it turns out, the leaves also have medicinal properties, making them useful to hunters. Scarlet Forest Florashell Crab Crabs that pile plants on their cup-like carapaces and burrow into the ground to hide from predators. Their claws are shaped like shovels, allowing them to burrow more easily. They generally remain buried during the day, becoming active only at night, where they can be found digging for insects to eat. Scarlet Forest Crudeshell Crab Crabs that pile oilsilt on their cup-like carapaces and burrow into the ground to hide from predators. Because oilsilt itself changes appearance depending on the season, it's theorized that these crabs must replace their silt to match. They are rarely seen in volcanic regions or during the Firespring, suggesting that they aren't particularly resistant to heat. Oilwell Basin Curioshell Crab Crabs that have retained their ancient appearance and are bigger than their standard brethren. They flee at full speed the instant they spot a foe, not bothering to camouflage themselves. Instead, they heap metal objects onto their carapaces, which are thought to be artifacts from ancient civilizations. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Oilwell Basin Iceshard Cliffs Ruins of Wyveria Smokeshroud Gekko Lizards with luminous organs around their body. When attacked. they go dark and leave luminous material in their wake as a decoy while they make a quick getaway. From this observation, it can be surmised that it's not their epidermis that glows, but some other substance stored in their bodies. In line with these habits, Smokeshroud Gekkos can generally be found in dark, gloomy places. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Dapperwing A species noted for its males, who don spectacularly colorful plumage after molting. During the Fallow, their plumage is a subdued green to match the vegetation, but once the Plenty arrives, males preen to the nines, adopting a vibrant color palette- presumably to court females, since the more flamboyant males with larger feathers seem to be the ones flapping away with a date. Scarlet Forest Dazzlewing Male Dapperwings with bigger tails, brighter plumage, and some pretty dam good odds when it comes to courting the females. Not only that, it is believed the plumage the males achieve after molting plays a vital role in what traits they pass on to their offspring. Scarlet Forest Mimiphyta Forest creatures that survive by using mimicry to disguise themselves as plants, confusing predators and luring in prey. Mimiphyta change form in response to seasonal and environmental changes; during the Fallow, they'll snuggle up at the base of a tree to live as fungi, while in the season of Plenty they blossom into lovely flowers and prey on butterflies and insects that flutter near. Scarlet Forest Scarlet Joyperch Creatures that have adapted to living both on land and in the water. They are precious evidence of how amphibious creatures might have come to walk the earth. Scarlet Joyperch become active at night, when they light up the luminous organs near their heads, spread their fins, and let out a cry- presumably their way of strutting their stuff to other members of their kind. Scarlet Forest Starduster Small fish with blue and yellow scales that prefer to live in schools. The sizes of these schools vary and at times can become impressively large. Because they both breed rapidly and exist in large numbers, they are frequently preyed upon, making them the backbone of the ecosystem. Splashes on the surface of bodies of water are a sign of this fish fleeing from large predators. Scarlet Forest Vaporonid Master weavers that use webs to ensnare prey. You will often see butterflies and other insects caught in their webs, which is as big as a person and is equipped with an escape route for the Vaporonid in case predators approach. The silk used in the web is extremely sturdy and comes in handy if you need to make pitfall traps to ensnare a few monsters of your own. Scarlet Forest Ruins of Wyveria Omenfly Insects whose bellies glow yellow or red when they detect threats. Their resemblance to fossils seems to suggest that Omenflies have been around for quite a long time. The glowing seems to be a warning to enemies. Since Omenflies stay near the water, it is believed they keep their larvae in the water or in the moist soil nearby. Adults will stay airborne for the majority of their lives. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Myriads Butterflies that hatch during the Plenty and pupate near its close. Those that survive the harsh Fallow emerge during the following season of Plenty. They adapt their life cycle to the Scarlet Forest, so if a population hatches early, it may reach adulthood within the same season. They feed a variety of organisms, including insectivorous plants, making them a crucial, non-predatory part of the ecosystem. Scarlet Forest Pink Landlight Shrimp Land-dwelling crustaceans that gather on gaseous vents in the Oilwell Basin. The hair on their heads and chests collects particles from the gas and steam, which attract the microbes that they feed on. The faint light they produce is thought to be a byproduct of the microbes they eat. They appear in a variety of sizes during the Firespring. Oilwell Basin Ember Rufflizard Lizards with distinctive creases on their heads. When they bob their heads to communicate, the crease opens and glows along with the pattern on their bodies. They prefer hot environments such as volcanic areas. Though rarely seen in the oilsilt- filled upper areas of the Oilwell Basin, they become far more active during the Firespring. It's theorized that they primarily feed on bugs that lurk among the rocks. Oilwell Basin Azure Rufflizard Rare lizards with a different coloration than the more common Ember Rufflizard. Since they so closely resemble the common Ember Rufflizard in every other way, they are currently thought to be a mere color variant as opposed to a subspecies. However, many reports claim these lizards are far larger, so they may be in the process of evolving into their own subspecies, a current hot topic among researchers. Oilwell Basin Gloom Gekko Lizards that prefer deep caves and other places that light doesn't easily reach. They can even survive in volcanic habitats, proof of their resilience in even the harshest of climes. Their toxicity is unknown, but the red and purple coloring of their skin is believed to be a warning. They survive by feeding on cave insects. Oilwell Basin Scalebat Creatures that greatly resemble bats. They hang from the ceiling in dark places like caves, and become active at night, preying on insects and smaller flying creatures. They can also be found in forests. Because of characteristics like their scales, it is theorized that they may have evolved from wyverns. Huge colonies live in caves in the Oilwell Basin, as evidenced by the piles of dung found there. Windward Plains Oilwell Basin Blink Angler Aquatic creatures that have adapted to life on land. Though similar to the Andangler, the shape of the light on their heads are different and are designed to attract the Sparklerjelly that float in the air of the Oilwell Basin. Due to this, some theorize that the Andangler and Blink Angler may have evolved from other amphibious fish. Windward Plains Oilwell Basin Sparklerjelly Jellyfish that float in the air. Often seen around the Coral Highlands in the New World. Environments that contain the airborne microbes they feed on and the air currents they use to get around are quite rare. Because the yellow ones found in the Oilwell Basin are all male and the larger, more colorful Peony Sparklerjelly are all female, it is theorized that they actually belong to the same species. Oilwell Basin Peony Sparklerjelly Jellyfish that float in the air. They are larger and more colorful than their yellow brethren. The tiny beads of light within them are actually eggs and larvae, so it's believed that these creatures are entirely female. This suggests that they belong to the same species as the yellow Sparklerjelly, which are entirely male. That being said, it's exceptionally rare for jellyfish to have such extreme differences between sexes. Oilwell Basin Nu Yaya The larva of a Nu Udra . Though a fully grown Nu Udra hadn't been seen in a long time, there have been glimpses of this larval form. Not much is known about their breeding cycles, but it seems many eggs will hatch at once, meaning groups of the creature are not uncommon. The distinctive pattern on their heads appears to hide an underdeveloped eyeball and seems to grow into better support of the eye. Oilwell Basin Gawkie Creatures that live in cold climates, often in abandoned buildings. Their slowness and lack of defensive measures suggest they aren't accustomed to threats. Perhaps because of this, they're not very cautious and will watch curiously while standing on their tails. They also like to gnaw on hard things like the Landspine to wear down their teeth. They are omnivorous today, but it's possible they fed on harder substances in the past. Iceshard Cliffs Oilwell Basin Solonaria Creatures with rippling fins on both sides of their body that help them move through the air. The fins possess a sort of circulatory system, similar to blood vessels, that give off light. Because their bodies are so fragile, even the slightest shock or gust of wind can tear them apart. Their regenerative abilities make up for this, however, as each piece becomes a separate individual. Ruins of Wyveria Pillowpod Creatures that survive by attaching to the Wyrmway, using energy-receptive organs along their abdomens to harvest its energy. This specialized adaptation, and its other traits that are consistent with ancient organisms, makes this species a fascinating subject of study. For example, their vision and nervous systems are highly developed for such a simple body structure, a detail that invites further investigation. Oilwell Basin Ruins of Wyveria Gillopod A special type of Pillopod with a larger body and prominent, expanded energy circulation system. Their eyes are compressed, suggesting a mutation caused by excessive energy intake. However, considering how little is known about ordinary Pillopods, these mysteries may take some time to elucidate. Ruins of Wyveria Petalfly Insects found in areas with ample vegetation such as the heart of the Ruins of Wyveria. Petalflies have poor flight capabilities, but they can still use the wind to get around. The larvae live in water or on moist ground until they mature, while an adult's short life span is spent reproducing. It has been suggested that the flowery fragrance the Petalflies give off may be a pheromone linked to mating. Ruins of Wyveria Xu Yo Xu Wu larvae, newly hatched from their eggs. They already give off a faint luminescence, and patterns can be seen through their thin, transparent skin. They generally move in groups, but not in an organized manner- it's only because they hatched at the same time. After hatching, the nutrients from their eggs sustain them for a while. Beyond that, their diet remains unknown. Ruins of Wyveria Jewel Scarab Brightly colored beetles with impressive shells that fan outwards. Each beetle's coloring and shapes are different, and with their sparkle, it's no wonder why they are likened to gems. they tend to be diurnal, gathering on trunks of trees in hopes of getting a nip of sap. Scarlet Forest Regal Jewel Scarab The finest of jewel scarabs specimens, with larger, more developed shells. They are found with the rest of their kind, feeding on sap, but are nearly impossible to spot. while jewel scarabs are known for variations in shape and color, the regale variations are harder to explain. are they a result of a particular larval diet, an acquired mutation, or are they even the same species. only more research will tell. Scarlet Forest Hercudrome Beetles with distinct horns extending from the head and pronotum. They are nocturnal and feed mainly on sap, which you can see if you observe tree trunks at night. The horns are used to compete for this food. They have wings, but their large size and heavy shells limit their flight capability. Since they buzz loudly while flying, you'll have an easier time finding them if you keep your ears open. Scarlet Forest Gold Hercudrome A Hercudrome subspecies with a shiny, golden carapace. Very few of these have been found, and no one has figured out why the carapaces are gold. However, the sightings span several regions and these are classified as a subspecies, not just a color variation. Because of their beauty, the are in high demand among collectors. Scarlet Forest Prism Hercudrome A member of the Hercudrome family with an iridescent carapace. Its pronotum is large and developed, with three prominent horns. Unlike ordinary Hercudromes, these inhabit the Ruins of Wyveria. Alas, there have been precious few sightings. Most have been at dawn or dusk, so that might be the best time to attempt and capture one. Ruins of Wyveria Downy Crake Birds that can be sighted riding on the backs of small monsters. They are extremely wary of external threats and are quick to flee, making them difficult to capture. Their disproportionately long legs allow them to move at high speeds. Populations have been observed living in some human settlements, allowing their eggs to be gathered in an extremely unusual symbiotic relationship. Iceshard Cliffs Aquatic Life [ ] Icon Name Description Location Virid Bowfin A species endemic to the Eastlands. They are more common in warm areas, suggesting that they have adapted to more temperate climates. Compared to other fish, Virid Bowfins are neither particularly useful nor suitable for eating, so anglers tend to treat them as bycatch. On the other hand, they have high scientific value because they exhibit unique traits not seen outside their Eastland habitat. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Sushifish A popular food source found in many a hunter's pouch, these fish have very high nutritional value-not just in their flesh, but their scales too. They offer a remarkable range of benefits, including health and bleeding recovery, and stamina boosts. Because of this, Sushifish are common prey for many aquatic species. They show a strong interest in Emerald Jitterbait. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Oilwell Basin Whetfish Fish covered with scales and fins of considerable durability. In fact, Whetfish are so hard that they are often used in place of common whetstones to sharpen weapons. It is believed these hardened features evolved as a defense mechanism against predators. They show a strong interest in Emerald Jitterbait. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Oilwell Basin Goldenfish With the stunning golden scales covering their bodies, it's no wonder these fish are aquarium staples and often sell for high prices for use in accessories. Goldenfish can be found in caves and other places with no sunlight and calm waters. Conversely, you will rarely see them out in the sun or in swift currents. They show a strong interest in Golden Bugheads. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Oilwell Basin Iceshard Cliffs Platinumfish Fish covered with platinum-colored scales. Like the Goldenfish, they are a popular feature in aquariums and accessories-but at higher prices, because of their relative scarcity. The leading theory for the Platinumfish's unique coloring is that they adapted to their sunless cave environments. They show a strong interest in Golden Bugheads. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Oilwell Basin Iceshard Cliffs Burst Arowana Fish with scales that detonate, a property that allows them to be crafted into things like bowgun ammo. How the Burst Arowana came to be this way is an unsolved mystery, and the debate rages on; some theorize it was an inherent trait, others that it was acquired through feeding habits. Burst Arowana show a strong interest in Emerald Jitterbait. Scarlet Forest Oilwell Basin Bomb Arowana Fish with scales that detonate even more powerfully than the Burst Arowana's, making them great material for potent bowgun ammo. The Burst and Bomb Arowana's relationship is disputed. Is the latter a subspecies? Should the difference be chalked up to habitat? Researchers are confident that this line of questioning will eventually reveal the fishes' explosive secrets. Bomb Arowanas show a strong interest in Emerald Jitterbait. Scarlet Forest Oilwell Basin Goldenfry Due to their sparkly appearance, Goldenfry are in high demand as aquarium fish, and their scales fetch a pretty zenny as well. Their distinctive traits-the short body, the long, broad fins-are not commonly seen in nature, leading some researchers to believe an anthropogenic factor might be at play. Goldenfry show a strong interest in Golden Bugheads. Scarlet Forest Oilwell Basin Iceshard Cliffs Ruins of Wyveria Gunpowderfish Fish that have explosive scales useful in bomb-making. The large, protruding eyes they have on both sides are rarely seen in nature, prompting some to theorize that the species evolved through human manipulation, only to become wild sometime later. Gunpowderfish show a strong interest in Emerald Jitterbait. Scarlet Forest Oilwell Basin Iceshard Cliffs Ruins of Wyveria Gravid Bowfin Fish with head plating to defend against threats. The plating is not part of their biology but rather a collection of scavenged materials. Because of the similar fin shapes, some believe these are actually female Virid Bowfins in their breeding period. However, not enough specimens have been found or studied to shed light on these theories. Gravid Bowfins show a strong interest in Emerald Jitterbait. Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Oilwell Basin Iceshard Cliffs Ruins of Wyveria Escunite Creatures with exceptionally hard shells that keep the majority of their bodies hidden from predators. Their numerous tentacles draw in and catch prey. Escunites move by expelling water from organs called siphons that are hidden inside their tentacles. They show a strong interest in Tentacle Jigs. Scarlet Forest Grand Escunite Escunite specimens with blanched bodies. Every part—shell, tentacles, and all—is white. Several theories have been put forward as to why this happens: a mutation, or a defensive measure to better blend into surroundings. However, until more specimens are collected and research conducted, it will remain as speculation. Grand Escunites show a strong interest in Tentacle Jigs. Scarlet Forest Glass Parexus Fish with transparent bodies that reveal their bones and organs. This protective coloration or lack thereof, rather-hides them from predators. Glass Parexus prefer them from predators. Glass Parexus prefer colder regions, and are often found in clear waters where their transparency is most effective. Iceshard Cliffs Ruins of Wyveria Blind Perch Fish that have adapted to waters where Wyvern Milk flows. Their eyes have degenerated, presumably to reduce energy consumption. In lieu of sight, Blind Perch have developed organs on the sides of their bodies to sense tremors in the water and gain awareness of their surroundings. What they use for nourishment still remains unknown. Iceshard Cliffs Ruins of Wyveria Gastronome Tuna Large fish that gobble up anything and everything they can swallow, including things a hunter might use for tools. Gastronome Tuna are commonly observed during the season of Plenty when smaller fish are abundant. Food is scarce during the Fallow, however, so sightings become rare as they move to more fruitful feeding grounds. They show a strong interest in Tuff Joint Bait. Scarlet Forest Goliath Squid Huge nocturnal creatures that have eight short legs, two long tentacles, and distinctive patterning on their bodies. Goliath Squids use special luminescent organs to light up their whole body, scaring off enemies and hunting prey. Some believe it might also be a way for them to communicate with each other. They show a strong interest in Tentacle Jigs. Scarlet Forest Speartuna Large fish characterized by the long rostra extending from their heads. The rostra are used to catch prey and defend against enemies. Speartuna are a common sight during the Fallow, but absent during the Plenty. It is believed that they avoid competing with other large fish over the abundant food. Speartuna show a strong interest in Tuff Joint Bait. Scarlet Forest Gajau Large fish with flat heads, broad mouths, and long whiskers. They are known to grow to a considerable size—some as large as a human. While an Eastland Gajau is wary and will flee from a perceived threat, there have been documented Gajau attacks in other parts of the world. Researchers link such differences in behavior to habitat. Gajau show a strong interest in Duster Rigs. Scarlet Forest Petricanths So few of these fish have been found alive that they were once thought to be extinct. At one point, they only turned up as fossils—and in a way, they are living fossils since they retain the same form as in ancient times. Research on the ecology of the Petricanths might just elucidate how other prehistoric organisms lived. Petricanths show a strong interest in Duster Rigs. Scarlet Forest Oilwell Basin Great Trevally Large fish notable for their sizeable mouths, which can gobble down most small- to medium- sized prey in one go. Great Trevally are diurnal and have a wide range of habitats. Smaller Specimens can even be found in the shallows. They show a strong interest in Tuff Joint Bait. Scarlet Forest Gallery [ ] Trivia [ ]

# MHWilds: Monster Material List

Chatacabra Scale+ 纏蛙の上鱗 6 850z A high-quality Chatacabra scale. Its enhanced durability makes it impervious to most blows. Chatacabra Hide 纏蛙の皮 4 300z The thick hide of a Chatacabra. Chatacabra Jaw 纏蛙の顎 4 360z The mandible plating of a Chatacabra. Hard protrusions stud the thick but supple leather. Chatacabra Jaw+ 纏蛙の大顎 6 1530z Fine plating from a Chatacabra's hulking jaw. Quematrice Igniter+ 炎尾竜の引火濃液 6 1566z Especially viscous fluid from a Quematrice. The gas it releases burns at a perilous temperature. Quematrice Crest 炎尾竜のトサカ 4 484z The crest of a Quematrice. So white and pristine that it could pass for a high quality pelt. Lala Barina Floret+ 刺花蜘蛛の上綿毛 6 900z High-quality hair from a Lala Barina. Its delicate, transparent strands dance in the wind. Lala Barina Shell 刺花蜘蛛の甲殻 4 375z The outer shell of a Lala Barina. Has a dark luster and is wonderfully lightweight. Lala Barina Carapace 刺花蜘蛛の堅殻 6 1350z The hard carapace of a Lala Barina. A bit of polish will make it shine black as night. Lala Barina Claw 刺花蜘蛛の爪 4 500z A claw from a Lala Barina. Vivid scarlet decorates its keen edge. Sharp as a sword, one thrust is all it takes to subdue prey. Lala Barina Stinger+ 刺花蜘蛛の上尾棘 6 1800z The razor-sharp stinger of a Lala Barina. Capable of piercing even the thickest armor. As red as fresh blood, it contains a powerful paralytic. Lala Barina Certificate 刺花蜘蛛の狩猟証 4 250z A Guild certificate awarded to those who have hunted a Lala Barina. Used to make equipment. Used to make equipment. -Click here to go back to the top- Congalala [ ] Icon Item Name Rarity Value Description Congalala Pelt 桃毛獣の毛 4 280z The pink fur of a Congalala. The tufts of fluffy hair are an ideal material for clothing. Congalala Pelt+ 桃毛獣の剛毛 6 930z High-quality Congalala fur. Stout and resilient, an important crafting material once processed. Congalala Claw 桃毛獣の爪 4 420z The long claw of a Congalala. Large and sharp, it gouges anything it touches. Congalala Claw+ 桃毛獣の尖爪 6 1395z A long, hard, sharp claw from the wildest of Congalala, and no less tricky to manage than its source. Congalala Fang 桃毛獣の牙 4 504z A Congalala incisor. While blunt, it is tough enough to crush even the hardest of substances. Congalala Fang+ 桃毛獣の鋭牙 6 1395z A long, hard, sharp claw from the wildest of Congalala, and no less tricky to manage than its source. Vibrant Pelt 極彩色の毛 4 616z Vibrant hair that grows on the head of a Congalala. The lovely colors are proof of its position in its pack. Gypceros Wing 毒怪鳥の翼 6 1450z A quality Gypceros wing membrane. Offers the same shock and poison protection—now with comfy stretch. Gypceros Head 毒怪鳥の頭 6 2090z The uniquely shaped head of a Gypceros. A must-have for Gypceros lovers! Gypceros Tail 毒怪鳥の尻尾 6 1900z The tail of a Gypceros. The most pliant body part, and thus the most difficult part to damage. Bird Wyvern Gem 鳥竜玉 7 4000z A valuable gem that can be harvested from bird wyverns. Said to fetch a high price on the market. Gypceros Certificate S 毒怪鳥の狩猟証Ｓ 6 950z A Guild certificate awarded to those who have hunted a Gypceros. Used to make equipment. -Click here to go back to the top- Rathian [ ] Icon Item Name Rarity Value Description Rathian Scale+ 雌火竜の上鱗 6 1200z A superior Rathian scale. Looks like a normal Rathian scale but is much harder. Rathian Carapace 雌火竜の堅殻 6 1800z Rathian carapace prized for its lavish green coloration. An excellent armor material. Rathian Webbing 雌火竜の翼膜 6 2160z A quality Rathian wing membrane. This heat-resistant material is often used as armor lining. Rathian Spike+ 雌火竜の上棘 6 2400z Rathian hairs, keen as a sword. Longer and sturdier than normal, they also contain more poison. Rathian Ruby 雌火竜の紅玉 7 7200z A rare, precious gem from inside a Rathian. Almost too nice to use. Rathian Certificate S 雌火竜の狩猟証Ｓ 6 1200z A Guild certificate awarded to those who have hunted a Rathian. Used to make equipment. Rath Medulla 火竜の延髄 6 3036z Only recently extractable due to advances in carving techniques. Hard to forge. -Click here to go back to the top- Guardian Fulgur Anjanath [ ] Icon Item Name Rarity Value Description Guardian Fulgur Scale+ 護雷顎竜の上鱗 6 1600z A high-quality Guardian Fulgur Anjanath scale. Surprisingly heavy, and zaps you when touched. Guardian Fulgur Pelt+ 護雷顎竜の上毛皮 6 2400z A high-quality Guardian Fulgur Anjanath pelt. Wild patterns have overshadowed its original form. Guardian Fulgur Fang+ 護雷顎竜の鋭牙 6 2800z A sharp fang from a Guardian Fulgur Anjanath. Built to withstand the fierce crushing power of its jaw. Guardian Fulgur Nosebone+ 護雷顎竜の上鼻骨 6 3520z The nasal bone of a Guardian Fulgur Anjanath. The mucus from it is charged with electricity. Guardian Fulgur Tail 護雷顎竜の尻尾 6 3200z A Guardian Fulgur Anjanath tail. Packed with thick muscles that help it maintain balance. Guardian Fulgur Gem 護雷顎竜の宝玉 7 9600z A rare jewel that developed within a Guardian Fulgur Anjanath. Said to hold the might of the heavens. Guardian Fulgur Certificate S 護雷顎竜の狩猟証Ｓ 6 1600z A Guild certificate awarded to those who have hunted a Guardian Fulgur Anjanath. Used to make equipment. -Click here to go back to the top- Rathalos [ ] Icon Item Name Rarity Value Description Rathalos Scale+ 火竜の上鱗 6 1380z An extraordinarily durable scale carved from the body of a Rathalos. Rathalos Carapace 火竜の堅殻 6 2070z This Rathalos carapace emanates a nobility befitting the King of the Skies. Rathalos Wing 火竜の翼 6 2484z The wing of a Rathalos. Skilled artisans can craft it into strong equipment. Rathalos Tail 火竜の尻尾 6 2760z A severed Rathalos tail covered in plates and keen spines. It has any number of uses. Rathalos Ruby 火竜の紅玉 7 8280z A rare, precious gem from inside a Rathalos. Almost too nice to use. Rathalos Certificate S 火竜の狩猟証Ｓ 6 1380z A Guild certificate awarded to those who have hunted a Rathalos. Used to make equipment. Rath Medulla 火竜の延髄 6 3036z Only recently extractable due to advances in carving techniques. Hard to forge. -Click here to go back to the top- Gravios [ ] Icon Item Name Rarity Value Description Gravios Carapace 鎧竜の堅殻 6 1460z The sturdy carapace of a Gravios. Fused with ore by intense heat, it has grown abnormally tough. Gravios Skull 鎧竜の頭殻 6 3212z The head armor of a Gravios, hardened by its own heat. Makes it hard to work with, too. Gravios Pleura+ 鎧竜の堅胸殻 6 2920z Shell that covers a Gravios's torso. It's grown so thick over time that average attacks do nothing to it. Gravios Medulla 鎧竜の延髄 7 8760z Though hard to extract and hard to craft, the brain stem is an especially precious Gravios part. Gravios Wing 鎧竜の翼 6 2190z The hefty wing of a Gravios. Recently, the demand and value of the material has been rising. Wyvern Gem 竜玉 7 4000z Throughout a wyvern's life, impurities gather and form this peculiar gem. Gravios Certificate S 鎧竜の狩猟証Ｓ 6 1460z A Guild certificate awarded to those who have hunted a Gravios. Used to make equipment. -Click here to go back to the top- Blangonga [ ] Icon Item Name Rarity Value Description Blangonga Pelt+ 雪獅子の剛毛 6 1320z Fine fur from a Blangonga. Its springy resilience and warmth make it prized in cold regions. Blangonga Fang+ 雪獅子の鋭牙 6 1980z A Blangonga canine. As large as a human thigh, it's basically a weapon already. Blangonga Whisker 雪獅子の髭 6 2904z A glorious mustache is the mark and pride of a Blangonga leader, a symbol of its might. Blangonga Tail 雪獅子の尻尾 6 2640z The tail of a Blangonga. The silky, lustrous coat is heavenly to the touch. Beast Gem 獣玉 7 4000z A valuable jewel formed from coagulated juices inside a monster. Glows wildly. Blangonga Certificate S 雪獅子の狩猟証Ｓ 6 1320z A Guild certificate awarded to those who have hunted a Blangonga. Used to make equipment. -Click here to go back to the top- Jin Dahaad [ ] Icon Item Name Rarity Value Description Jin Dahaad Scale+ 凍峰竜の上鱗 6 2300z A high-quality Jin Dahaad scale. Its metallic properties keep it from freezing. Jin Dahaad Carapace 凍峰竜の堅殻 6 3450z The tough carapace of Jin Dahaad. Tough as steel without impeding movement. Jin Dahaad Iceplate 凍峰竜の凍成殻 6 4140z From the back of a Jin Dahaad, this plate's metallic properties allow it to freeze the surrounding air. Jin Dahaad Claw+ 凍峰竜の尖爪 6 4600z A sharp claw from a Jin Dahaad. Terrifyingly cold, it freezes everything it touches. Jin Dahaad Horn 凍峰竜の層角 6 5060z A layered horn from a Jin Dahaad. Also acts as a heat vent. Jin Dahaad Tail 凍峰竜の尻尾 6 5060z The tail of a Jin Dahaad. Has a cooling organ that continues to make cold air even when severed. Jin Dahaad Icegem 凍峰竜の氷玉 7 13800z A rare jewel developed within a Jin Dahaad. Said to wield the power to freeze entire cliffs. Jin Dahaad Certificate S 凍峰竜の狩猟証Ｓ 6 2300z A Guild certificate awarded to those who have hunted a Jin Dahaad. Used to make equipment. -Click here to go back to the top- Gore Magala [ ] Icon Item Name Rarity Value Description Gore Magala Scale+ 黒蝕竜の上鱗 6 2330z A deep black scale that seems to devour surrounding light, not reflect it. Gore Magala Carapace 黒蝕竜の堅殻 6 3450z The Gore Magala's handsome black suit of armor, said to negate all blows. Gore Magala Ripclaw+ 黒蝕竜の鋭惨爪 6 4140z A claw from a Gore Magala's second foreleg. It inflicts Frenzy and generations of suffering. Gore Magala Wing+ 黒蝕竜の上暗翼 6 4140z A horrifying wing that casts a hungry shadow over prey and drives them insane. Gore Magala Feeler+ 黒蝕竜の尖触角 6 5060z A well-honed sensory organ from a Gore Magala that oozes violet melancholy from its infected tip. Gore Magala Tail 黒蝕竜の尻尾 6 4600z A pliant tail covered in reverse scales that strikes like a whip, not a blade. Gore Magala Nyctgem 黒蝕竜の闇玉 7 13800z A pure, jet-black jewel that imprisons and swallows all nearby light. Gore Magala Certificate S 黒蝕竜の狩猟証Ｓ 6 2300z A Guild certificate awarded to those who have hunted a Gore Magala. Used to make equipment. -Click here to go back to the top- Arkveld [ ] Icon Item Name Rarity Value Description Arkveld Scale+ 鎖刃竜の上鱗 6 3000z A high-quality Arkveld scale. Despite being incredibly dense and hard, the edges chip easily. Arkveld Armorplate 鎖刃竜の堅鎧殻 6 4500z A durable Arkveld carapace. Named for how its scales overlap like plate armor. White Arkveld Pelt 鎖刃竜の白毛 6 5400z A white Arkveld pelt. Strong, naturally twining strands create extra protection against attacks. Arkveld Calloushell 無慈悲な鎖刃甲 6 6000z A linked Arkveld carapace that is connected by flexible sinew, allowing it to move like a chain. Arkveld Horn+ 鎖刃竜の尖角 6 6600z A sharp, pointed Arkveld horn. So menacing, its mere sight dispirits any who must stand against it. Arkveld Tail 鎖刃竜の尻尾 6 6000z The tail of an Arkveld. Highly prized for the thick muscle hidden beneath its skin and hair. Arkveld Gem 鎖刃竜の宝玉 7 18000z A mysterious gem that formed inside an Arkveld. Its pulsating white light seems to absorb life. Arkveld Certificate S 鎖刃竜の狩猟証Ｓ 6 3000z A Guild certificate awarded to those who have hunted an Arkveld. Used to make equipment. -Click here to go back to the top- Mizutsune [ ] Icon Item Name Rarity Value Description Mizutsune Scale+ 泡狐竜の上鱗 6 1750z A high-quality Mizutsune scale. Older scales polished over time can be very valuable. Mizutsune Purplefur+ 泡狐竜の紫上毛 6 3550 High-quality purple Mizutsune fur. More foam-absorbent than its lighter counterpart. Mizutsune Claw+ 泡狐竜の尖爪 6 3500z Friction-reducing bubblefoam helps this deadly claw slice through prey like sushi. Mizutsune Fin+ 泡狐竜の上錦ヒレ 6 3500 A high-quality Mizutsune fin. Bubblefoam gives it an elegant pattern prettier than dye. Mizutsune Tail 泡狐竜の尻尾 6 3850z A flowing Mizutsune tail. It's prized for its supple fur and beautiful scales. Bubblefoam+ 泡立つ上滑液 6 3150z The top layer of the Mizutsune fluid. Removes impurities and is used in many rituals. Mizutsune Water Orb 泡狐竜の水玉 7 10500z A multicolored orb formed from a crystalized Mizutsune scale and bubblefoam. Mizutsune Certificate S 泡狐竜の狩猟証Ｓ 6 1750z A Guild certificate awarded to those who have hunted a Mizutsune. Used to make equipment. -Click here to go back to the top- Zoh Shia [ ] Icon Item Name Rarity Value Description Zoh Shia Crystalscale 白熾龍の結晶鱗 6 3200z A crystallized Zoh Shia scale. Its dull glow evinces the great energy it contains. Zoh Shia Layershell 白熾龍の層殻 6 4800z The coiled nature of Zoh Shia's layered shell looks as though it protects as much as it constricts. Zoh Shia Stakeclaw 白熾龍の冊状爪 6 5760z Stake-like Zoh Shia claws extended by hardened Wylk. The Wylkcrystal growths are eerily uniform. Zoh Shia Crystalhorn 白熾龍の晶角 6 6400z A crystal-coated Zoh Shia horn. Heavy and wedge-like, as though it were used to seal something. Zoh Shia Crystaltail 白熾龍の晶尾 6 7040z A crystal-covered Zoh Shia tail. The crystals continue to grow even after the tail is severed. Blackember Mass 黒熾の瘤塊 6 6400z A small, swollen mass from the inside of Zoh Shia. It pulses with an alien-like quality. Faux Whitegleam Orb 白耀の造龍玉 4 19200z An inorganic orb from inside Zoh Shia. Something wriggles deep within its elaborate design. Zoh Shia Certificate S 白熾龍の狩猟証Ｓ 6 3200z A Guild certificate awarded to those who have hunted Zoh Shia. Used to make equipment. -Click here to go back to the top- Lagiacrus [ ] Icon Item Name Rarity Value Description Lagiacrus Scale+ 海竜の上鱗 6 2200z A high-quality Lagiacrus scale. Both its robustness and beautiful blue hue are truly breathtaking. Lagiacrus Hide+ 海竜の上皮 6 3300z A fine hide taken from a Lagiacrus. It's quite strong and immensely flexible. Lagiacrus Shockspike 海竜の背電殻 6 3960z The spike from a Lagiacrus's back. Safe to handle since it's used to release electricity, not generate it. Lagiacrus Horn+ 海竜の尖角 6 4400z A fine horn taken from a strong Lagiacrus. Merely having one on hand is enough to draw attention. Lagiacrus Tail 海竜の尻尾 6 4840z The tail of a Lagiacrus. Used not only in weaponcraft, a certain village also considers it a delicacy. Lagiacrus Claw+ 海竜の鋭爪 6 4400z The sharp claw of a Lagiacrus. Though unremarkable at a glance, a single strike from it can be fatal. Lagiacrus Sapphire 海竜の蒼玉 7 13200z A deep azure gem formed inside a Lagiacrus. The sound of rippling water can be heard from its core. Lagiacrus Certificate S 海竜の狩猟証Ｓ 6 2200z A Guild certificate awarded to those who have hunted a Lagiacrus. Used to make equipment. -Click here to go back to the top- Seregios [ ] Icon Item Name Rarity Value Description Seregios Slavescale 千刃竜の鋭刃鱗 6 2200z A sharp scale the Seregios can make stand on end. Gouges anything it touches. Seregios Airblade 千刃竜の飛刃 6 3300z A blade shot from the body and carried on the wind toward unsuspecting hunters. Seregios Carver 千刃竜の刻爪 6 3960z Hooked hind leg talons that tear through armor and flesh without mercy. Seregios Scraper 千刃竜の尖翼爪 6 4400z A wing talon. Most attacks come from the hind legs; the wings serve to shift weight. Seregios Breacher 千刃竜の伐刀角 6 4400z A sharp horn like a hefty axe that, not surprisingly, makes for fine equipment. Seregios Impaler 千刃竜の槍尾 6 4840z A spear-like tail covered in blade-like scales. Capable of truly horrific things. Seregios Dissenter 千刃竜の反逆鱗 7 13200z A single scale that points defiantly in a different direction from its brethren. Seregios Certificate S 千刃竜の狩猟証Ｓ 6 2200z A Guild certificate awarded to those who have hunted a Seregios. Used to make equipment. -Click here to go back to the top- Omega Planetes [ ] Icon Item Name Rarity Value Description Omega Screw オメガの螺子 6 3200z A puzzling screw from Omega Planetes. Made with such precision and complexity that it cannot be replicated. Omega Spring オメガの発条 6 4800z A puzzling spring from Omega Planetes. Its detailed makeup allows it to bear unusually heavy loads. Omega Pedal オメガの機脚 6 5760z A puzzling pedal from Omega Planetes. Its incredible technological capabilities allow it to move many parts at once. Omega Lens オメガの透鏡 6 6400z A puzzling lens from Omega Planetes. It's said that peering through it allows you to see into a new dimension. Omega Axle オメガの廻軸 6 6400z A puzzling axle from Omega Planetes. Interdimensional technology allows it to rotate with immense force. Omega Nodule オメガの赤熱核 7 19200z A puzzling core from Omega Planetes. It pulsates with heat, as if still active even after being removed from the main body. Omega Data 1.0 オメガデータ1.0 6 3200z Data left by Omega Planetes. Includes records of the countless battles it has had with various heroes. -Click here to go back to the top- Gogmazios [ ] Icon Item Name Rarity Value Description Gogmazios Rampart 巨戟龍の塁鱗 7 3400z A scale covered in the hardened drakesbone oil Gogmazios continually secretes. Gogmazios Fortalice 巨戟龍の砦殻 7 5100z A carapace studded with weapons and armaments. No halfhearted attack can so much as faze it. Gogmazios Lacerator 巨戟龍の大刃 7 6120z A twisted blade slicked with blood and oil. Its dull edge crushes instead of cutting. Gogmazios Shredders 巨戟龍の大爪 7 6120z Red-hot claws the Gogmazios uses to cruelly sear prey within its long arms' reach. Gogmazios Fiberwing 巨戟龍の筋繊翼 7 6800z A great wing too tattered to raise winds, sending instead vicious splatters of oil. Gogmazios Thorntail 巨戟龍の刺鋲尾 7 7480z A long, vicious tail lined with countless spines. Strikes fear into all who look upon it. Wartorn Dragongem 戦火の龍玉 8 20400z A precious gem formed within a Gogmazios's body. At its core is a firestorm capable of consuming all. Heavy Drakesbone Oil 超重質龍骨油 7 5100z Hazardous fluid that courses through the Gogmazios's body and explodes at high heat. Gogmazios Certificate S 巨戟龍の狩猟証Ｓ 7 3400z A Guild certificate awarded to those who have hunted a Gogmazios. Used to make equipment. -Click here to go back to the top-

# MHWilds: Assigned Quests

MHWilds: Weapons ・ MHWilds: Armors ・ MHWilds: Monsters ・ MHWilds: Monster Material List ・ MHWilds: Palico Gear ・ MHWilds: Locations ・ MHWilds: Skills ・ MHWilds: Item List ・ MHWilds: Assigned Quests ・ MHWilds: Optional Quests ・ MHWilds: Event Quests ・ MHWilds: Endemic Life List ・ MHWilds: Decorations ・ MHWilds: Charm List Contents 1 Low Rank 1.1 1 Star 1.2 2 Stars 1.3 3 Stars 2 High Rank 2.1 4 Stars 2.2 5 Stars 2.3 6 Stars 2.4 7 Stars 2.5 8 Stars 2.6 9 Stars Low Rank [ ] 1 Star [ ] Slimy Situation 纏蛙チャタカブラの討伐 Main Objective : Slay the Chatacabra Location : Windward Plains Season : Fallow Reward : 3960z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR1+ Monsters: Chatacabra Description : To ensure the safety of the people in this area, the Guild requests that you slay this Chatacabra. If you feel overwhelmed, use an SOS Flare to call for backup. - Alma Hot on their Tails 火花散るケマトリス狩猟 Main Objective : Hunt the Quematrice Location : Windward Plains Season : Fallow Reward : 3960z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR1+ Monsters: Quematrice Description : We've spotted an SOS Flare from the Support Team and have confirmed that a Quematrice is after their goods. At this rate, the monster will follow that tasty meat straight to Base Camp! You have to stop them! - Palico A Stage of Rose and Thorn 赤い舞台と花の刺 Main Objective : Hunt the Lala Barina Location : Scarlet Forest Season : Fallow Reward : 3960z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR2+ Monsters: Lala Barina Description : Erik of the Astrum Unit was rescued after being captured by a Lala Barina. It is likely to retaliate over the loss of its prey. Please go hunt them to avoid further victims. - Alma Drive Off the Congalala! ババコンガを追っ払え！ Main Objective : Hunt the Congalala Location : Scarlet Forest Season : Fallow Reward : 4320z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR2+ Monsters: Congalala Description : Big creature is dis-dressing honey mountain. Yes yes, pink fur is prettyful — but smoke has the stink. BIG stink. You drive creature away, we give you puny hovel! - Alma (On behalf of Plumpeach) Balahara of the Sandsea 砂海原のバーラハーラ Main Objective : Hunt the Balahara Location : Windward Plains Season : Fallow Reward : 4320z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR2+ Monsters: Balahara Description : The Vanguard leader was injured by Balahara while fleeing. The monsters unfortunately picked up his scent, which will lead them straight to Kunafa. Please go hunt them, for everyone's safety. - Alma Pinnacle of the Pack 闢獣の頂に座す Main Objective : Hunt the Alpha Doshaguma Location : Windward Plains Season : Sandtide Reward : 5760z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR2+ Monsters: Doshaguma Description : A pack of Doshaguma is closing in on Kunafa. Hunt the alpha and the rest should scatter. Please — for everyone's safety. - Alma 2 Stars [ ] A Feast in the Deep 水底の宴 Main Objective : Repel the Uth Duna Location : Scarlet Forest Season : Downpour Reward : 8280z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR3+ Monsters: Uth Duna Description : Spotted unknown monster beneath the water during investigation. Was careless. Fought to protect crew, but became target. Identifiable by its water-like veils. Requesting assistance to drive off the monster. - Olivia A Fuse Ignited 熱き心は爆発寸前？ Main Objective : Hunt the Rompopolo Location : Oilwell Basin Season : Fallow Reward : 5760z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR3+ Monsters: Rompopolo Description : It's highly likely that a girl of the Oilfolk has wandered into monster territory. Please go hunt the Rompopolo, for everyone's safety. - Alma A Merciless Glare 慈悲無き閃光 Main Objective : Repel the Rey Dau Location : Windward Plains Season : Sandtide Reward : 8280z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR3+ Monsters: Rey Dau Description : A monster — suspected to be the desert's apex predator — and the White Wraith have been spotted atop Wind's Gasp. The apex predator began to rampage in the wake of the White Wraith's absence. The monster must be slain for the safety of the village. - Erik The Quiet, Cunning Assassin 沈黙、狡猾、暗殺者の調べ Main Objective : Hunt the Nerscylla Location : Iceshard Cliffs Season : Frostwinds Reward : 6120z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR4+ Monsters: Nerscylla Description : It's that giant spider. So this is my death... Nay, life would be preferred. Oh, has a new death come for me? Or is it my salvation? I shall leave it to fate. - Alma (On behalf of Rove) Fight or Flight ヒラバミの狩猟依頼 Main Objective : Hunt the Hirabami Location : Iceshard Cliffs Season : Frostwinds Reward : 6480z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR4+ Monsters: Hirabami Description : Avis Unit member Nata has come down with a fever. Hunt the Hirabami to clear the path to Azuz in order to get him back safely. - Alma Ajarakan Ablaze 激熱！赫猿獣アジャラカン Main Objective : Hunt the Ajarakan Location : Oilwell Basin Season : Firespring Reward : 6480z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR4+ Monsters: Ajarakan Description : Mending the Landspine has caused Inclemency to occur in Oilwell Basin and now a number of Ajarakan are attacking Azuz. Please go hunt them to ensure the village's safety. - Alma 3 Stars [ ] The Black Flame Manifests 黒炎はある日静かに訪れる Main Objective : Hunt the Nu Udra Location : Oilwell Basin Season : Firespring Reward : 9000z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR4+ Monsters: Nu Udra Description : Inclemency has caused havoc in the Everforge, summoning a monster clad in dark flames from the depths of Oilwell Basin. Please hunt this monster to ensure the village's safety. - Alma An Ominous Encounter 不穏の開闢 Main Objective : Slay the Guardian Doshaguma Location : Ruins of Wyveria Season : Wyvern's Stirring Reward : 7920z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR5+ Monsters: Guardian Doshaguma Description : We encountered a white Doshaguma while searching the Keepers' Vigil. That is, it looked like a Doshaguma, but its behavior was... strange. Was it really a Doshaguma? - Alma The Ashen King 白き王者 Main Objective : Slay the Guardian Rathalos Location : Ruins of Wyveria Season : Wyvern's Stirring Reward : 7560z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR5+ Monsters: Guardian Rathalos Description : It was a Rathalos, king of the skies, that attacked the door we suspect leads to the Village of the Keepers! It appears to have similar characteristics to the white Doshaguma we found previously. Slay this monster before it brings harm to the Keepers. - Alma Repel the Guardian Arkveld 護竜アルシュベルドの撃退 Main Objective : Repel the Guardian Arkveld Location : Ruins of Wyveria Season : Wyvern's Stirring Reward : 9720z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR5+ Monsters: Guardian Arkveld Description : The White Wraith, Guardian Arkveld. The mysteries surrounding this monster run deep. For now, we must prioritize the search and rescue of two missing Keepers. Guardian Arkveld must be driven off. - Alma The Rimechain Trial 氷鎖の試練 Main Objective : Repel the Jin Dahaad Location : Rimechain Peak Season : N/A Reward : 9720z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR5+ Monsters: Jin Dahaad Description : We must get to Suja and locate the Allhearken, but a huge monster freezing the peaks of the mountain is blocking the path. It must be driven off to ensure the safety of all. - Alma Watcher of the Ancient Capital 古都の番人 Main Objective : Slay the Guardian Ebony Odogaron Location : Ruins of Wyveria Season : Wyvern's Stirring Reward : 7560z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR6+ Monsters: Guardian Ebony Odogaron Description : While surveying the ecology within Wyveria, we encountered an extremely territorial Guardian Ebony Odogaron. Fleeing or hiding from this beast is not an option. The Guild requests that you slay it to ensure the safety of the area. - Alma A Hunter in the Void 狩人は虚無に潜む Main Objective : Hunt the Xu Wu Location : Ruins of Wyveria Season : Wyvern's Stirring Reward : 8640z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR6+ Monsters: Xu Wu Description : Wyveria is home to a monster that preys upon Guardians. Please go hunt this monster for everyone's safety and our continued research of this ecosystem. - Alma The Pale Dream of a Lone Shadow 孤独な影の白い夢 Main Objective : Slay the Guardian Arkveld Location : Ruins of Wyveria Season : Wyvern's Awakening Reward : 9720z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR6+ Monsters: Guardian Arkveld Description : Now that the Guardian Arkveld has learned how to feed, it has gone berserk and is attacking everything in sight. It must be slain in order to restore balance to the ecosystem. - Alma Guardian Zoh Shia 護竜ゾ・シア Main Objective : Slay the Zoh Shia Location : Dragontorch Shrine Season : Reward : 10080z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR7+ Monsters: Zoh Shia Description : The Guardian that has slept for eons in the thrall of the Dragontorch now teeters on the edge of awakening. The time to slay Zoh Shia has come. - Hunter High Rank [ ] 4 Stars [ ] Lured by Abundance 豊穣に集う者たち Main Objective : Capture the Yian Kut-Ku Location : Scarlet Forest Season : Plenty Reward : 12960z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR8+ Monsters: Yian Kut-Ku Description : A group of Yian Kut-Ku has been sighted in the Scarlet Forest that has entered the season of Plenty. Unfortunately, one was hit by a Wudwud paintball, and now they're all on alert. Please capture the specific monster for the sake of our investigation. - Alma Investigating the Frenzied Shadow 狂おしまは影の調べ Main Objective : Hunt the Frenzied Nerscylla Location : Wounded Hollow Season : Reward : 18000z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR15+ Monsters: Frenzied Nerscylla Description : There's been a sighting of a Nerscylla thought to be infected with the Frenzy in Wounded Hollow. Please hunt it quickly before it endangers other areas of the Forbidden Lands. - Alma 5 Stars [ ] Thundering Flowers 美麗に舞う花雷 Main Objective : Hunt all target monsters Location : Ruins of Wyveria Season : Wyvern's Stirring Reward : 35640z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR20+ Monsters: Guardian Fulgur Anjanath and Tempered Lala Barina Description : A tempered Lala Barina has caused a Guardian Fulgur Anjanath to emerge from its cocoon. Is this a natural occurrence here in Wyveria, I wonder? Whatever the case may be, the Guild's mission is clear: hunt both monsters. - Olivia The Entrancing Water Dancer 淡紅の泡狐がたゆたうか Main Objective : Hunt the Mizutsune Location : Scarlet Forest Season : Plenty Reward : 19080z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR21+ Monsters: Mizutsune Description : A Mizutsune has been sighted nearby the Wudwuds' fishing hole. Unfortunately, it appears they share the same diet, resulting in a food shortage for the Wudwuds. A hunt has been authorized to ensure the safety of the locals. - Alma 6 Stars [ ] Frozen Lord on the Precipice 断崖断絶を住くは凍土の主 Main Objective : Slay the Jin Dahaad Location : Rimechain Peak Season : Reward : 21960z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR30+ Monsters: Jin Dahaad Description : Jin Dahaad activity has been confirmed as the cause of the frozen Wyrmway in Oilwell Basin. According to Nata's report, we're lucky to have discovered this before the damage spread further. Slay it before it starts affecting other areas. - Alma Roaring Rey Dau 轟くは煌雷 Main Objective : Hunt the Rey Dau Location : Windward Plains Season : Sandtide Reward : 20520z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR30+ Monsters: Rey Dau Description : We sighted a Rey Dau on a plateau not far out. Even from afar, its fury was clear to see. It was terrifying... So we decided to evacuate the village, before anything happened. - Alma (On behalf of Zatoh) Tide Overtaking Timber 波音は森を越えて Main Objective : Hunt the Uth Duna Location : Scarlet Forest Season : Downpour Reward : 20160z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR30+ Monsters: Uth Duna Description : Big rain, fall, fall, fall. Water go WHOOSH! Whole forest BIG wettings! Lord of forest, different. Big angry! More water go WHOOSH! Tim work together. Copper-ate! - Alma (On behalf of Sassafrill) Scorching the Earth Once More 黒炎は再び地を焦がす Main Objective : Hunt the Nu Udra Location : Oilwell Basin Season : Firespring Reward : 20160z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR30+ Monsters: Nu Udra Description : That thing... The Black Flame. It's just outside the village. By swallowing the valley's fire, it's grown far hotter than before — and I'm afraid it's something we simply cannot handle. - Alma (On behalf of Maki) Shrouded in Black 黒き衣を纏う竜 Main Objective : Slay all target monsters Location : Iceshard Cliffs Season : Frostwinds Reward : 45720z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR30+ Monsters: Gore Magala and Frenzied Blangonga Description : Gore Magala was found inhabiting in the Iceshard Cliffs. We were able to corner it with the help of Rex and Cobb, but as its existence could prove calamitous to the native environment, we must neutralize it. - Fabius Wings That Rend the Sky 空裂く翼 Main Objective : Slay the Seregios Location : Windward Plains Season : Plenty Reward : 19800z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR31+ Monsters: Seregios Description : That Seregios is not happy about its prey being taken away. In time, it may attempt to track it down, putting everyone in Kunafa Village at risk. For the safety of the settlement, the Guild authorizes you to slay that Seregios! - Alma Notes : Unlocks the &#160; Vanguard's Glaive . Tremors on Land, Sea, and Forest 海と陸と森の共震 Main Objective : Capture the Lagiacrus Location : Scarlet Forest Season : Plenty Reward : 19800z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR31+ Monsters: Lagiacrus Description : What is the Lord of the Seas doing in the waters of the Scarlet Forest!? Given the circumstances, it doesn't appear to be native to the area. Capturing a Lagiacrus is no small feat, but there is no one more capable than you, Hunter! - Erik 7 Stars [ ] Awakening From a Dream 夢から覚めて Main Objective : Slay Arkveld Location : Windward Plains Season : Sandtide Reward : 22320z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR40+ Monsters: Arkveld Description : For the safety of the surrounding regions, you have been authorized to slay Arkveld. Based on all the reports of destruction caused by this monster, it is of the utmost threat. Approach with extreme caution. - Nata A Covert Check 影なるレベルチェック Main Objective : Slay the Nerscylla Clone Location : Iceshard Cliffs Season : Fallow Reward : 20160z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR41+ Monsters: Nerscylla Clone Description : The creature that looks like a baby Seikret and the artifact that resembles the Mega 17 have been tied up, probably by a Nerscylla. To ensure their safety, the guild authorizes you to slay the Nerscylla - but it's acting a bit strangely... - Alma 8 Stars [ ] Invader from the Stars 星惑いの侵攻者 Main Objective : Slay the Omega Planetes Location : Rimechain Peak Season : N/A Reward : 30240z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR41+ Monsters: Omega Planetes Description : "*blip* An act of selfless courage... Are you a hero, then? If so, this battle is not unwinnable. Now, go - let them see the strength with which they dare trifle." "Kweh kweh!" - Omega & Alpha Germination 萌芽 Main Objective : Slay the Zoh Shia Location : Dragontorch Shrine Season : N/A Reward : 22680z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR50+ Monsters: Zoh Shia None Description : Zoh Shia has somehow reared its head once more. While it's unclear how this happened, or how the Dragontorch is involved, the situation must be resolved with utmost haste. I hope I can once again entrust this matter to the Avis Unit. - Olivia 9 Stars [ ] Ghosts Pay No Heed to Tomorrow 亡霊は明日の戦禍を顧みぬ Main Objective : Slay the Gogmazios Location : Forgotten Machineworks Season : N/A Reward : 39600z Time Limit : 50 mins. Requirements : HR100+ Monsters: Gogmazios None Description : Gogmazios, an elder dragon, has been discovered in the Forbidden Lands. It falls to all hunters to utilize the Guild's every resource, slay this threat, and protect the ecosystem at all costs. - Fabius
