# Doshaguma

Doshaguma ドシャグマ &#160; (Doshaguma) Carves · Equipment · Gallery · Videos Ecology English Title Pioneer Beast Japanese Title 闢獣 Chinese Name 辟兽 (Simplified) 闢獸 (Traditional) Korean Name 도샤구마 Korean Title 벽수 General Information Monster Class Fanged Beast Elements None Ailments None Weakest to Fire Thunder Habitat Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Ruins of Wyveria Wounded Hollow Size 1,807.61 1,237.51 Related Monsters Guardian Doshaguma Arzuros Goss Harag Generation Sixth Doshaguma is a Fanged Beast first introduced in Monster Hunter Wilds . Contents 1 Physiology 2 Behavior and Abilities 3 Game Appearances 3.1 Main Series 4 In-Game Description 5 Notes Physiology [ ] Doshaguma is a large monster with a bear-like gait. It is covered in thick golden-brown fur, with the area surrounding the back of its skull and the peak of its spine being noticeably shaggier and similar in appearance to a lion's mane. Similar to Arzuros, Doshaguma has armored forelegs and bear-like paws and claws, though not as heavily armored as the former's with only several small scutes and bumps being present. Its face is wrinkled, slightly reddish in hue and completely devoid of fur, with two large oval-shaped bony pads on the top of its cranium. Its eyes are noticeably smaller than any other monsters. It also possesses distinctive tusk-like canines on both jaws, with the tusks on the lower jaw protruding upwards being slightly larger in comparison. Its tail is long and covered in flowing brown fur as well. It seems to be similar in size and appearance to another Fanged Beast, Goss Harag . Alpha Doshagumas are noticeably larger, more aggressive and more powerful, with faces that are a brighter shade of red as well as having thicker reddish colored manes. Behavior and Abilities [ ] Doshaguma are said to be very aggressive and territorial. It is also found in a wide-range of habitats. Despite these aggressive traits, Doshaguma can actually form small packs and attack their enemies as a group, usually with a single alpha and two subordinates. The reason why is yet unknown, though it is theorized that it is to allow them to take down prey that they would not be able to on their own. However, Doshaguma's pack behavior is not as much cohesive, but more like they are just sticking together to get a chance of free meal when one of the pack member took down a prey. They can additionally stand on their hind legs and roar loudly, akin to other Fanged Beasts, to appear larger and more threatening to their opponents. Similar to the Odogaron they have a habit of fighting anything regardless of the other monster's power giving them a berserker type mentality. Doshaguma in battle mainly rely on their brute strength, using their powerful claws and paws to swipe, slash and smash opponents to the ground. Along with biting opponents with their fangs, they also like to charge at hunters with their jaws wide open to try and devour them. Doshaguma can also pick up and throw the corpses of their prey at hunters as an additional attack, often times, spikeless Ceratonoths . Game Appearances [ ] Click on Expand to see contents. Main Series [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds (Introduced) In-Game Description [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds Fanged beasts that are sometimes seen traveling in packs. Ruggedly built and extremely aggressive, Doshaguma won't hesitate to throw everything they've got at enemies - even other small monsters. They have been known to take prey back to their dens, which sometimes leads to violent disputes. Threat Level (危険度): ★-★★★★★ Notes [ ] General General Notes The name Doshaguma is a combination of dosha (kanji: 土砂 / kana: ドシャ), Japanese term for 'earth and sand', and guma , the rendaku form of kuma (kanji: 熊 / kana: クマ) which means "bear". It has turf wars with Balahara and Lala Barina. It is shown that one Doshaguma dominates over 3 Balaharas in a turf war. Doshaguma can be power clashed by blocking its claw slams or bites. It was also the monster used in promotional material to introduce the mechanic. The Doshaguma is very reminiscent of the Bandersnatch from Tim Burton's Alice in Wonderland. Alpha Doshagumas will always be a large gold crown. After taking enough damage, it will expose several wounds on its body, and let out a high pitched roar. After this happens it will start using more aggressive attacks, and won't stagger as easy when wounds are destroyed. It is one of the few monsters that are referred to by name by the inhabitants of the Forbidden Lands. It is the first large monster introduced in Wilds, and serves as a placeholder before Arkveld's reveal. It is first seen in a pack of 8, which is the largest for large monsters; however, this is only in a story quest, as normally they would be in packs of 3. ▷ Monsters appearing in Monster Hunter Wilds Gelidron Bird Wyvern &#160; Gypceros &#160; Kranodath &#160; Porkeplume &#160; Talioth &#160; Yian Kut-Ku Brute Wyvern &#160; Quematrice &#160; Rompopolo Cephalopod &#160; Nu Udra &#160; Xu Wu # Gore Magala

Gore Magala ゴア・マガラ &#160; (Goa Magara) Carves · Equipment · Gallery · Videos Ecology English Title Black Eclipse Wyvern Dark Eclipse Japanese Title 黒蝕竜 災厄の黒き蝕 Chinese Name 黑蚀龙 (Simplified) 黑蝕龍 (Traditional) Chinese Title 灾厄黑蚀 (Simplified) 災難之黑蝕 (Traditional) Korean Name 고어 마가라 Korean Title 흑식룡 재앙의 검은 침식 General Information Monster Class ??? &#91; 1 &#93; (4th Gen, 5th Gen, and Frontier Z) Elder Dragon &#91; 2 &#93; Demi Elder (6th Gen Onwards) Elements Earth ( MHXR ) Ailments Frenzy Virus Weakest to Fire Thunder Dragon Ice ( MHXR ) Habitat Deserted Island Ancestral Steppe Primal Forest Everwood Sanctuary Tower Tower 3 Great Sea Jurassic Frontier Ruined Pinnacle Shrine Ruins Flooded Forest (MHRise) Citadel Jungle (MHRS) Iceshard Cliffs Ruins of Wyveria Wounded Hollow Painted Waterfalls Size 2201.12 ~ 1549.59 Related Monsters Chaotic Gore Magala Tenth Angel Gore Magala Shagaru Magala Risen Shagaru Magala Generation Fourth Gore Magala is a Demi Elder (originally introduced as ??? type ) introduced in Monster Hunter 4 . It is the juvenile form of Shagaru Magala . Contents 1 Physiology 2 Abilities 3 Behavior 4 Habitat 5 Other Non-Subspecies Forms 5.1 Chaotic Gore Magala 5.2 Tenth Angel Gore Magala 6 Game Appearances 6.1 Main Series 6.2 Frontier 6.3 Spin-offs 7 In-Game Description 8 Music Themes 9 MH4U Breakable Parts 10 Notes 11 References Physiology [ ] Gore Magala is a very unique monster, sharing traits and similarities to that of other Elder Dragons , possessing six limbs, including the clawed wings on its back. This is because it is an immature elder dragon itself. Its overall appearance and stance resembles a quadrupedal wyvern. Its body is covered in dark plates, with notable features including the hidden feelers that are folded alongside its face, the lack of visible eyes and fanged jaws that are actually parts of its external armor plates. The other unique part is its wings, which are covered in jet-black fur that resemble a tattered and ragged cape. The claws on its wings are extremely prehensile, and even seem to possess opposable thumbs. They are used for grabbing, help at running and maintaining stability. When not engaged in combat, Gore Magala tends to cloak its body with its wings by latching them onto its back. Abilities [ ] Gore Magala emits pollen-like scales that are used to understand their environment and leave behind a trail that stick to predators and prey, allowing Gore Magala to sense them via heat. These scales also act as the vector for the pathogen known as the Frenzy Virus , which Gore Magala can also transmit with its volatile breath attacks or by scattering pools of its scales around the area. Gore Magala does not have visible eyes and relies almost exclusively on its scales to detect threats. As it scatters more scales, Gore Magala's senses will gradually become sharper, and the color under its wings will slowly change and get brighter. When its senses are at their highest, two feelers will unfurl from its head and it will release large amounts of scales into the air. The hairs will darken the sky as if an eclipse was in the area and it will begin to walk on all six. This state is known as the Frenzy State, and is characterized by a tremendous increase in strength and aggression from Gore Magala's maximized perception. Outside of its Frenzy Virus, Gore Magala primarily fights enemies using powerful slashes and grapples from its muscular clawed wingarms, which can easily pin down large monsters such as Goss Harag . In Monster Hunter Frontier Gore Magala Is an Exotic Species and gains a new rage mode known as True Frenzy . In this state its wings will change shape and Frenzy Crystals will grow across its feelers and neck. It will attack much more ferociously and perform new attacks. Only the strongest Gore Magala can achieve this state. Behavior [ ] Gore Magala are highly aggressive monsters that will brutally attack nearly anything they encounter. However, they are also quite intelligent, as they can sometimes play dead to escape dangerous situations, and have been observed to hold grudges against individuals who injure them or drive them off. They are especially aggressive when it is almost time for them to molt into Shagaru Magala . Habitat [ ] Gore Magala is known for inhabiting places like the Ancestral Steppe , Jurassic Frontier , The Citadel , Forest and Hills , Primal Forest , Heaven's Mount , Sanctuary , Everwood , Ruined Pinnacle . Other Non-Subspecies Forms [ ] Chaotic Gore Magala [ ] Main Article: Chaotic Gore Magala Chaotic Gore Magala is a rare special Gore Magala individual that has failed to molt properly into a Shagaru Magala and has only molted half way on one side of its body. It also has a different music theme which has parts of Gore Magala's and Shagaru Magala's respective theme music as well as the Apex theme. This Variant of Gore Magala first appeared in Monster Hunter 4 Ultimate with a separate Icon, yet in game it does not have a separate name from an ordinary Gore Magala. Tenth Angel Gore Magala [ ] Main Article: Tenth Angel Gore Magala A Special Species of Gore Magala first appearing in Monster Hunter Explore . Game Appearances [ ] Click on Expand to see contents. Main Series [ ] Monster Hunter 4 (Introduced) Monster Hunter 4 Ultimate Monster Hunter Generations Monster Hunter Generations Ultimate Monster Hunter Rise: Sunbreak Monster Hunter Wilds Frontier [ ] Monster Hunter Frontier Z Monster Hunter Frontier Z Zenith Spin-offs [ ] Monster Hunter Spirits Monster Hunter Explore In-Game Description [ ] Monster Hunter 4 多くの謎に包まれた”黒蝕竜”。神出鬼没で、特徴的な翼膜をなびかせて飛来する姿が目的されている。外敵を察知した際など、己の感知能力を高める鱗粉をまき散らす。他の生物がこれを吸いこんでしまうと、身体能力に大きな影響を及ぼす。 Threat Level (危険度): ★★★★★ Monster Hunter 4 Ultimate A curious monster that, when threatened, scatters pollen-like scales that heighten its own senses while causing detrimental effects to any other creatures that inhale them. As elusive as they are mysterious. Threat Level (危険度): ★★★★★ Monster Hunter Generations / Generations Ultimate A bizarre wyvern that, when threatened, scatters pollen-like scales that heighten its own senses while causing detrimental effects to any other creatures that inhale them. As elusive as it is mysterious. Threat Level (危険度): ★★★★★ Monster Hunter Rise: Sunbreak Gore Magala possess wings with black, shroud-like membrane. They lack visual receptors, heightening their senses by scattering scales from their wings to ascertain the locations of lifeforms. At the peak of their senses, feelers extend from their head, their wings unfurl, and they become frenzied. They're feared not only for their sinister appearance but also for the mysterious Frenzy virus they carry. Threat Level (危険度): ★★★★★★★ Monster Hunter Wilds Monsters that drape themselves in their great wings, like a black overcoat. They have no eye sensory organs, so instead they use a scattering of fine scales from their wings to locate prey. When their sensory capacity increases, antennae appear on their heads and they unfurl their wings, entering the aggressive Frenzy State. Their black scales can Frenzy other creatures as well, making them fearsome adversaries. Monster Hunter Frontier Z (?) Music Themes [ ] MH4: Gore Magala Music Theme MH4U Breakable Parts [ ] Face (x1) Feelers (x1) Wings (x1) Wingarms (x1) Tail (Sever) Notes [ ] General MH4U MHGen MHRise MHWilds Frontier MHXR General Notes The Gore in Gore Magala's name was added to infer an evil and sinister monster, whereas Magala translates loosely to "incarnation" in Aramaic, thus Gore Magala loosely means "gore incarnate". &#91; 3 &#93; Gore Magala is the first fourth generation monster to have its localized English name revealed. The name was first revealed by Yuri of Capcom-Unity in the January 27, 2014 episode of Monster Hunter Mondays, as the name is used on multiple official Japanese merchandise even before the announcement of Monster Hunter 4 Ultimate. This is a similar case with Zinogre back in Monster Hunter Portable 3rd . Gore Magala is the first flagship monster to be capable of infecting other monsters. It is also the first "juvenile" flagship monster, and the first flagship monster to lack visible eyes. Gore Magala has some common features of an Elder Dragon . In particular, having six limbs, including the wings on its back. It also has some features in common with the quadrupedal flying wyverns, like the Nargacuga , some of its scales, notably the ones on its wings, have turned into a fur-like substance. The purple colored patch where its eyes should be resemble the heat-detecting patches on a Gigginox . Gore Magala's Frenzy Virus can spread to hunters (either directly or via the attacks of monsters that have succumbed to infection), which cancels natural recovery and severely reduces defense as well as causing the hunter to lose health if standing in a virus pool after a short incubation time. However, if the hunter inflicts a certain amount of damage on the monster who infected them (Or any other monster.) before the incubation time is completed, the virus will be purged and the hunter will receive a large bonus to their affinity and be immune to the virus for a limited time. Frenzy Virus causes lowered resistance and abnormalities in the nervous system to any organisms that inhale Gore Magala's scales or hairs. When in Frenzy Virus mode, the surrounding area becomes much darker. Gore Magala's roar requires HG Earplugs to block. Gore Magala bears some resemblance to the Xenomorphs of the famous "Aliens" franchise. Gore Magala's roar has an eerie resemblance to the wailing and screaming of people. Over time its perceptive ability will increase. This is indicated by the coloring on its wings changing from blue to purple. Gore Magala's wings act like a large cape when it's not enraged. According to one of the Uniqlo Monster Hunter 10th Anniversary T-Shirts , the size of the foot of a Gore Magala is 135 centimeters, while its additional limbs are 241 centimeters. A Gore Magala appears in the comic crossover Sonic the Hedgehog/Mega Man: Worlds Unite , first appearing in issue #10 of the comic Sonic Boom . Although Gore Magala is the juvenile version of Shagaru Magala , it is slightly larger than its adult variant. Before Monster Hunter Wilds introduced the Demi Elder classification, external official sources, such as the "Hunter's Encyclopedia 4," placed Gore Magala in the Elder Dragon classification as the same species as Shagaru Magala. Despite this, Gore Magala can be trapped and remains a ??? in Monster Hunter 4 , Monster Hunter 4 Ultimate , Monster Hunter Generations , Monster Hunter Generations Ultimate , Monster Hunter Rise: Sunbreak , and Monster Hunter Frontier Z . Gore Magala was voted 5th place in the Hunters' Choice for Top Monster during Monster Hunter's 20th Anniversary. &#91; 4 &#93; Monster Hunter 4 Ultimate In the cinematic intro Gore Magala is seen attacking and killing a Tigrex . Gore Magala will feed on Gargwa and Slagtoth to regain its stamina. Gore Magala's head, feelers (Frenzy Virus mode only), wings and wingarms can be broken, and its tail can be severed and carved. It can spread the Frenzy Virus around its legs, infecting hunters who come in contact with its hairs and scales but not in Frenzy Virus mode. When in Frenzy Virus mode, its "Shadow shot" attack becomes a AOE attack. Gore Magala can be knocked out of its Frenzy Virus mode similar to a fully charged Zinogre . It will also drop a shiny. Gore Magala's feelers will not glow as brightly as normal if it is close to being knocked out of Frenzy Virus mode as the glow starts to fade. When exhausted, Gore Magala will start drooling a black fluid. Gore Magala will also fail to shoot balls of the Frenzy Virus. However, it can still damage you if you get too close to its mouth. When weak, Gore Magala will sleep in area 9 in the Ancestral Steppe . When facing a higher or lower platform, its "wingarms" will cling onto the platform. Gore Magala will only enter its Frenzy Mode if hunters who are engaging it remain infected by the Frenzy Virus for long enough. Because of this, it is possible that Gore Magala will not get an opportunity to enter its Frenzy State at all. Monster Hunter Generations Gore Magala will now enter its Frenzy State at least once a hunt. Monster Hunter Rise Gore Magala's feelers are now referred to as antenna Gore Magala can fire a frenzy ball to a far distance. When knocked out of the Frenzy mode, Gore Magala gets knocked down for a short amount of time. It has a turf war with Seregios, a call-back to their major roles in 4 Ultimate. It gain a hipcheck attack. Monster Hunter Wilds It has been assigned a new classification, Demi Elder, likely due to it being a juvenile elder dragon. The introduction cutscene calls back to Monster Hunter 4's opening cinematic by having it kill a Frenzied Yian Kut-Ku by breaking its back, which Gore did with Tigrex in the former game. Gore Magala's wings can now be broken individually, though for unknown reasons, its head and wingarms are unbreakable. It can be drained by Arkveld in a turf war fashion. At launch, Gore Magala could only appear in the Iceshard Cliffs and the Wounded Hollow , but its Tempered form was silently added to the Ruins of Wyveria in Title Update 1. Monster Hunter Frontier Gore Magala is an Exotic Species (遷悠種) Gore Magala gains a new rage mode known as True Frenzy . In this state its wings will change shape and spikes will grow across its feelers and neck. It will attack much more ferociously and perform new attacks. Monster Hunter Explore Even with the game saying that Gore Magala has the earth element, it doesn't seems to use any elemental attack. # Gravios

Gravios グラビモス &#160; (Gurabimosu) Carves · Equipment · Gallery · Videos Ecology English Name Gravios English Title Armor Wyvern Japanese Title 鎧竜 Chinese Name 铠龙 (Simplified Chinese) 鎧龍 (Traditional Chinese) Korean Name 그라비모스 Korean Title 개룡 General Information Monster Class Flying Wyvern Elements Fire Ailments Fireblight (4th Gen Onward) Sleep Poison (6th Gen) Weakest to Water Habitat Old Swamp Old Volcano Swamp Volcano Primal Forest Volcanic Hollow Everwood Ingle Isle Marshlands Ruined Pinnacle Oilwell Basin Ruins of Wyveria Wounded Hollow Interceptor's Base Sky Corridor Dark Veil Forest Ghost Rune Volcanoes Babda Rainforest Dovan Volcano Terga Bountiful Dunes Rococo Rocks Size 3065.9 1049.35 Related Monsters Zenith Gravios Black Gravios Basarios Crystal Basarios Ruby Basarios Sandstone Basarios Gureadomosu Generation First Appearances Main Series MH1 , MHG , MHF1 , MH2 , MHF2 , MHFU , MH4 , MH4U , MHGU , MHWilds Spin-Offs MHFrontier , MHO Stories MHST1 , MHST2 , MHST3 , MHRiders Crossovers TEPPEN Gravios is a Flying Wyvern introduced in Monster Hunter . Contents 1 Physiology 2 Abilities 3 Behavior 4 Habitats 5 Other Non-Subspecies Forms 5.1 Hard Core (HC) Gravios 6 In-Game Description 7 MH4U Breakable Parts 8 Notes Physiology [ ] Gravios is covered in a dense, stone-like armored shell which protects its body from physical damage. It is very large in size, towering over most other wyverns and measuring several dozen feet in length. Because of its size and incredible weight, it is only capable of limited flight, despite having large and seemingly-developed wings. It has a thick tail with a mace-like club at the end. Abilities [ ] While the Gravios seems to have lost its poisonous gas that the premature form, Basarios, possesses, gaps in the Gravios' underside expel a knockout gas potent enough to put many monsters to sleep. Furthermore, these defense mechanisms may be used to deter would-be predators, although there would be few monsters able to penetrate the Gravios's shell. Gravios can also expel flammable gas as a waste product of its 'heat beam', and of its diet. Behavior [ ] Gravios is relatively docile until provoked. Like most wyverns, it is fiercely territorial. The Gravios inhabits large areas of the Volcano, where it is sufficiently large enough to incubate and raise its juvenile Basarios . Habitats [ ] Gravios can be found in the Volcano and Swamp . It can easily withstand the heat of molten lava, and traverse it at will. Gravios eats rocks and minerals for nutrients. Other Non-Subspecies Forms [ ] Hard Core (HC) Gravios [ ] It appears to be an older Gravios than usual due to further growth of spikes and muscles. This orange eyed living tank has an improved high speed beam sweep that, once charged, is able to sweep across in only 1 second making it both easy to avoid when timed correctly and deadly if not evaded. The beam is so strong the Gravios itself almost falls over. It also has a new charge attack where it steps back while turning towards its intended target to then charge forward by jumping and sliding instantly at high speed finished by slamming the ground. It also has a new tail attack where it walks forward then lifts its tail in the air slowly as if charging a attack then swinging it fast and violently, causing great damage. In-Game Description [ ] Monster Hunter Freedom A large wyvern found in volcanic zones. They possess a fiery breath attack and the ability to emit a sleeping gas. They also have extremely hard scales that deflect most weapon attacks. Monster Hunter Freedom 2 / Monster Hunter Freedom Unite A large wyvern found in volcanic zones. They possess a fiery breath attack and the ability to emit a sleeping gas. They also have extremely hard scales that deflect most weapon attacks. Monster Hunter 4 火山地帯に生息する大型の飛竜。 強力な高熱ブレスに加え、体から睡眠ガスを排出する。 外殻として発達した骨は非常に堅い。 この外殻が破壊されると、意外な脆さを露呈することも。 Threat Level (危険度): ★★★★★ Monster Hunter 4 Ultimate Large wyverns found in volcanic areas. They possess a fiery breath attack and the ability to emit sleeping gas. Their bony carapaces are extremely tough, but expose a surprisingly brittle interior once destroyed. Threat Level (危険度): ★★★★★ Monster Hunter Generations Ultimate Large wyverns found in volcanic areas. They possess a fiery breath attack and the ability to emit sleeping gas. Their bony carapaces are extremely tough, but expose a surprisingly brittle interior once destroyed. Threat Level (危険度): ★★★★★ Monster Hunter Wilds Large wyverns found in Oilwell Basin. They have a developed physique, powerful heat rays, and a host of gases they can emit from their body. Their bony carapaces are famously tough but expose a suprisingly brittle interior once destroyed. Monster Hunter Stories Flying Wyverns that inhabit volcanic regions. Capable of attacking with fiery breath and sleeping gas. Monster Hunter Stories 2: Wings of Ruin Flying Wyverns that inhabit volcanic regions. Capable of attacking with fiery breath and sleeping gas. The carapace-like bone they have developed on their bodies for protection are incredibly hard. Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection A large Flying Wyvern found in torrid regions. It possesses a sturdy carapace and likes to consume minerals that contain explosive properties, such as volcanic rocks or Firestones. Monster Hunter Frontier 火山地帯に生息する大型の飛竜。 強力な高熱ブレスに加え、体から睡眠ガスを排出する。 外殻として発達した骨は非常に堅い。 この外殻が破壊されると、意外な脆さを露呈することも。 Monster Hunter Online 分类 飞龙种，岩龙的成年个体。 生境范围 主要栖息在火山、森丘。 适应性 岩龙在经过漫长的生长之后，会逐渐呈现出其最终成长完整的形态，这便是铠龙。 “如果说岩龙是一块巨大的岩石，那么铠龙就是一座巨大的山丘。”一位经验丰富的老猎人，曾经这样意味深长的说道。由此可见，铠龙光从体型上来说已经庞大到一种令人咋舌的地步。 铠龙体型巨大，然而它的性格却异常温顺，除非是为了保卫领地，通常不会主动攻击人类。然而人类与铠龙之间的争斗却没有停歇过，从一个侧面来说，如何与这些庞然大物共存，也是人类文明在不断前行过程中不得不面对的一个问题。 行为 由于常年吞食矿石，铠龙的体表也呈现出矿石化的特征，变得坚硬异常。而铠龙吞食的矿石，在其体内经过细菌的作用，也变为易燃的气体储存起来。当遭受外敌攻击时，铠龙可以通过释放这些易燃气体，喷吐出猛烈的热射线来攻击对手。同时它的体表上还有许多气孔，多余的高温气体可以通过这些气孔排出体外，灼伤靠近的敌人。 MH4U Breakable Parts [ ] Head (x1) Chest (x2) Back (x1) Right Wing (x1) Left Wing (x1) Right Leg (x1) Left Leg (x1) Tail (Sever) Notes [ ] General MHFU MH4U MHWilds Frontier MHST2 MHST3 General Notes Gravios can put its enemies to Sleep with a gas emitted from pores around its body. The stomach can be broken twice - the first will reveal the monster's muscle layers, while the second makes the break in its stomach larger. Once this happens, the stomach will receive more damage. When its legs receive a great deal of damage, it will roll over, dealing damage to whatever it hits. Gravios will also roll over when KO'd. It can be determined which kind of beam it will do by the sound of its voice. When it is higher pitched, its fire beam will go side to side. At some time in battles, Gravios may seemingly fly up. However, it should be noted that there is no wind effect as with other monsters, and it will follow up by slamming its body down on the ground, inflicting some medium damage and creating a wide area quake effect. The fire beam can be blocked using the Guard Increase skill - however, it cannot be completely blocked even when using Guard +2 and Guard Increase simultaneously - the hunter who has blocked the strike will still take damage. Using Flash Bombs can blind a Gravios for roughly 10-15 seconds. Gravios' roar requires HG Earplugs to block. Its roar when becoming enraged only requires Earplugs. In the first and second generations (barring Freedom Unite ), the red gas that follows the beam is less predictable, as the smoke cue preceding the red gas was not added until Unite. Gravios was voted 139th place in the Hunters' Choice for Top Monster during Monster Hunter's 20th Anniversary. &#91; 1 &#93; It tied with Black Gravios with 139th place. Monster Hunter Freedom Unite The red gas that comes after the beam can be predicted, as the Gravios will emit small black smoke around its body. Gravios can use Poison gas in the quest "Armored Supremacy". ( https://youtu.be/LgoKKBns5eY ). This however is likely a bug, as Gravios is not meant to be able to use this status. Monster Hunter 4 Ultimate Gravios' back, legs, wings,&#160;and head can broken for the first time. When one of Gravios' wings is broken, it will roll over after the hopping body slam attack. When Gravios' back is broken, it will no longer release fire gas from its belly after shooting a heat beam. It will instead release it upwards from the holes on its back. Gravios has a unique mounting sequence where the hunter will instead mount on its stomach. This is initiated when Gravios' back is broken or when it is exhausted. When low on stamina it will fail to shoot its heat beams, produce gas, and take longer to recover from attacks. In G-rank, Gravios can shoot heat flares at random targets while walking. It can become infected by the Frenzy Virus and affected by the Apex Status. Apex Gravios's weak spots are its legs, torso and tail tip. Attacking any other part will cause weapons to bounce and shots to be deflected, unless the Drive Wystone is active. Apex Gravios uses G-rank Black Gravios's moveset. Apex Gravios is unlocked in G3 Elder Hall after obtaining the G-rank special permit by hunting a combined total of 15 Gravios and Black Gravios. There is an Event Quest where you can hunt a small Gravios which is about the size of a Basarios. Monster Hunter Wilds Gravios uses its entire moveset from prior games, including the Black Gravios super-beam. It is now able to use poison gas, assumably due to Basarios not being included in Wilds. This also makes it the second game where Basarios and Gravios are not both present; in Rise it was Gravios that was absent. Instead of its wings like previous games, breaking one of Gravios' legs will cause it to roll over after a hopping body slam attack. After certain attacks, the used part can be focus struck to cause a stagger, but only if said part isn't broken. Noticeably, Gravios makes stock bear sounds during certain actions, including its enrage roar. Monster Hunter Frontier Gravios can be a pet. Monster Hunter Stories 2 Gravios is a Power type monster. Opposing Gravios’ turn to Technical attacks when enraged. Breaking each part with a Big Barrel Bomb will boost its retreat rate. Like the baby Black Diablos and baby Deviants in the game, baby Gravios is a paradox since baby Gravios should be Basarios . In Double Kinship attacks, Gravios can be seen flying. Monster Hunter Stories 3 Gravios is shown flying in cutscenes. ▷ Monsters appearing in Monster Hunter ( edit ) Herbivore &#160; Aptonoth &#160; Apceros &#160; Kelbi &#160; Mosswine Fanged Beast &#160; # Guardian Arkveld

Guardian Arkveld 護竜アルシュベルド &#160; (Gādian Arushuberudo) Carves · Equipment · Gallery · Videos Ecology English Title Guardian Chain Blade Wyvern Japanese Title 護鎖刃竜 Chinese Name 护锁刃龙 (Simplified) 護鎖刃龍 (Traditional) Korean Name 수호룡 알슈베르도 Korean Title 호쇄인룡 General Information Monster Class Construct Elements Dragon Ailments Dragonblight Weakest to Dragon Habitat Ruins of Wyveria Size 1,666.54 Related Monsters Arkveld Generation Sixth The Guardian Arkveld is a Construct based on Arkveld , first introduced in Monster Hunter Wilds . Contents 1 Physiology 2 Behavior and Abilities 3 Game Appearances 3.1 Main Series 4 In-Game Description 5 Materials 6 Notes Physiology [ ] Guardian Arkveld is nearly identical to its standard counterpart, with glowing veins covering its head, body, forelimbs, and chainblades. It appears more white in comparison to normal Arkveld. Like other Guardians, it has solid black eyes. Behavior and Abilities [ ] Like other Guardians, Guardian Arkveld can draw power from Wyvern Milk, but also enhance its own abilities by draining power from monsters. This led to it evolving the ability and desire to prey on other Guardians. Driven into a frenzy by an endless thirst for Wyvern Milk and developing a feeding instinct that it could not possibly benefit from, it became a threat to the ecosystem of the Ruins of Wyveria and had to be put down. Guardian Arkveld's chainblade attacks release explosions that create Wylkrystals on pools of Wyvern Milk. During the final battle, it gains the ability to use Dragon element attacks after feeding from Guardian Seikret pods. Game Appearances [ ] Click on Expand to see contents. Main Series [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds (Introduced) In-Game Description [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds An artificial species created by an ancient civilization and imbued with the power of a monster called Arkveld. It cannot reproduce and does not require food, relying instead on Wyvern Milk. In addition to absorbing and discharging energy the way other Guardians do, Guardian Arkveld can also absorb elemental energy through the chainblades on its wings and use it for sustenance. Materials [ ] This section is about monster materials obtained from hunting Guardian Arkveld in Monster Hunter Wilds . Low Rank Carves Name Chance Rarity Price Guardian Scale 50% Rarity 4 500z Contains an unknown substance. It has lost any color it previously had and possesses unique properties. Guardian Pelt 30% Rarity 4 500z Contains an unknown substance. It has lost any color it previously had and courses with energy. Guardian Pelt 20% Rarity 4 1100z Guardian blood containing an unknown substance. A valuable research material. Guardian Blood 100% (Tail Carve) Rarity 4 1100z Guardian blood containing an unknown substance. A valuable research material. Quest Rewards Name Chance Rarity Price Guardian Scale 30% Rarity 4 122z Can be obtained from some large monsters. Has the ability to amplify the effects of certain medicines. Guardian Pelt 20% Rarity 4 500z Contains an unknown substance. It has lost any color it previously had and courses with energy. Guardian Scale 20% Rarity 4 500z Contains an unknown substance. It has lost any color it previously had and possesses unique properties. Tough Guardian Bone 20% Rarity 4 600z A bone found in the Ruins of Wyveria. Its peculiar shape is unique to the local wildlife. Guardian Blood 10% Rarity 4 1100z Guardian blood containing an unknown substance. A valuable research material. Guardian Pelt 100% (Break) Rarity 4 500z Contains an unknown substance. It has lost any color it previously had and courses with energy. Guardian Scale 100% (Head Break) Rarity 4 500z Contains an unknown substance. It has lost any color it previously had and possesses unique properties. Guardian Pelt 100% (Break Right ou Left Chainblade) Rarity 4 500z Contains an unknown substance. It has lost any color it previously had and courses with energy. Guardian Scale 45% (Wound Destroyed) Rarity 4 500z Contains an unknown substance. It has lost any color it previously had and possesses unique properties. Guardian Pelt 43% (Wound Destroyed) Rarity 4 500z Contains an unknown substance. It has lost any color it previously had and courses with energy. Guardian Blood 12% (Wound Destroyed) Rarity 4 1100z Guardian blood containing an unknown substance. A valuable research material. Notes [ ] General General Notes Guardian Arkveld serves as the penultimate boss of the Low Rank storyline. Guardian Arkveld currently cannot be refought. The materials to create its equipment can be obtained from other Guardians and its base species in High Rank. Until the first title update Zoh Shia also was a one time only hunt. Guardian Arkveld is the only Construct Monster to be based on an Extinct Species . Guardian Arkveld can be power clashed by blocking its bite or chainblade slams. Guardian Arkveld's weapons are named after the Knights of the Round Table from Arthurian mythos. The same holds true for its base species counterpart. Guardian Arkveld can sometimes be seen feeding on Guardian cocoons in the Ruins of Wyveria by simultaneously piercing the cocoon with its chainblades to drain its energy, while devouring the hibernating Construct inside. Guardian Arkveld was added to Final Fantasy XIV as part of the collaboration between it and Monster Hunter Wilds in October 2025. &#91; 1 &#93; ▷ Monsters appearing in Monster Hunter Wilds ( edit ) # Guardian Doshaguma

Guardian Doshaguma 護竜ドシャグマ &#160; (Gādian Doshaguma) Carves · Equipment · Gallery · Videos Ecology English Title Guardian Pioneer Beast Japanese Title 護闢獣 Chinese Name 护辟兽 (Simplified) 護闢獸 (Traditional) Korean Name 수호룡 도샤구마 Korean Title 호벽수 General Information Monster Class Construct Elements None Ailments Defense Down Weakest to Fire Ice Thunder Dragon Habitat Ruins of Wyveria Size 1,724.18 1,251.42 Related Monsters Doshaguma Generation Sixth Guardian Doshaguma is a Construct based on Doshaguma , first introduced in Monster Hunter Wilds . Contents 1 Physiology 2 Behavior and Abilities 3 Game Appearances 3.1 Main Series 4 In-Game Description 5 Notes Physiology [ ] Compared to regular Doshaguma, Guardian Doshaguma have white fur with prominent glowing veins covering their head and forearms. Behavior and Abilities [ ] Like other Guardians, Guardian Doshaguma can harness the power of Wyvern Milk to enhance and sustain themselves, gaining greater power. Their claw attacks release powerful explosions that create Wylkrystals if they come into contact with pools of Wyvern Milk. Their wounds regenerate if not quickly destroyed. Unlike the regular Doshaguma, their saliva is corrosive and inflicts Defense Down. They are also aggressive to everything in their way and will attempt to destroy it. Like the regular Doshaguma, Guardian Doshaguma are aggressive towards its own species, but unlike the regular species, it does not do so to establish a pack hierarchy. Game Appearances [ ] Click on Expand to see contents. Main Series [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds (Introduced) In-Game Description [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds An artificial monster species created by an ancient civilization and imbued with Doshaguma's power. Guardian Doshaguma, cannot reproduce and do not require food, relying instead on Wyvern Milk for energy. While anything but agile, they're tough enough to charge right through attacks without flinching, making them extremely dangerous. Notes [ ] General General Notes Repeatedly destroying Guardian Doshaguma's wounds before they can regenerate will topple it and deplete its power, rendering it temporarily unable to cause explosions or regenerate wounds. The next time it changes areas, it will absorb energy, leaving itself vulnerable for an extended period of time. Guardian Doshaguma can be power clashed by blocking its claw slams or bites. Guardian Doshaguma has a Turf War with Xu Wu and Guardian Fulgur Anjanath . ▷ Monsters appearing in Monster Hunter Wilds ( edit ) Herbivore &#160; Ceratonoth &#160; Dalthydon &#160; Rafma Neopteron # Guardian Ebony Odogaron

Guardian Ebony Odogaron 護竜オドガロン亜種 &#160; (Gādian Odogaron Ashu) Carves · Equipment · Gallery · Videos Ecology English Title Guardian Wicked Claw Wyvern Japanese Title 護兇爪竜 Chinese Name 护凶爪龙 (Simplified) 護兇爪龍 (Traditional) Korean Name 수호룡 오도가론 아종 Korean Title 호흉조룡 General Information Monster Class Construct Elements Dragon Ailments Dragonblight Bleeding Weakest to Water Habitat Ruins of Wyveria Size 1,722.05 1,222.10 Related Monsters Ebony Odogaron Odogaron Generation Sixth Guardian Ebony Odogaron is a Construct based on Ebony Odogaron , first introduced in Monster Hunter Wilds . Contents 1 Physiology 2 Behavior and Abilities 3 Game Appearances 3.1 Main Series 4 In-Game Description 5 Notes Physiology [ ] Guardian Ebony Odogaron have distinctive glowing veins over their body in addition to white patterns on their body and forelegs. They have solid black eyes. Behavior and Abilities [ ] Like other Guardians, Guardian Ebony Odogaron can harness the power of Wyvern Milk to enhance and sustain themselves, gaining greater power. Their claw and tail attacks release powerful explosions that create Wylkrystals if they come into contact with pools of Wyvern Milk, which they can then detonate using Dragon attacks. Their wounds regenerate if not quickly destroyed. Game Appearances [ ] Click on Expand to see contents. Main Series [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds (Introduced) Monster Hunter Wilds: Ascendance In-Game Description [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds An artifical monster species created by an ancient civilization and imbued with Ebony Odogaron's power. Guardian Ebony Odogaron cannot reproduce and do not require food, relying instead on Wyvern Milk for energy. They will attempt to corner targets with flurries of attacks from their well-developed claws and tails. Notes [ ] General General Notes Repeatedly destroying Guardian Ebony Odogaron's wounds before they can regenerate will topple it and deplete its power, rendering it temporarily unable to use Dragon attacks, cause explosions, or regenerate wounds. The next time it changes areas, it will absorb energy, leaving itself vulnerable for an extended period of time. Unlike its organic counterpart, it cannot be toppled by elemental damage to its head, and said part can be broken without the need of elemental attacks or depowering it. Alongside Guardian Fulgur Anjanath , Guardian Ebony Odogaron are the only Construct monsters to be based on Subspecies . They are also the first subspecies to appear without their base counterpart, albeit as a Guardian, and the first to appear in Low Rank since Monster Hunter Freedom Unite. ▷ Monsters appearing in # Guardian Fulgur Anjanath

Guardian Fulgur Anjanath 護竜アンジャナフ亜種 &#160; (Gādian Anjanafu Ashu) Carves · Equipment · Gallery · Videos Ecology English Title Guardian Thunder Jaw Wyvern Japanese Title 護雷顎竜 Chinese Name 护雷颚龙 (Simplified) 護雷顎龍 (Traditional) Korean Name 수호룡 안쟈나프 아종 Korean Title 호뢰악룡 General Information Monster Class Construct Elements Thunder Ailments Thunderblight Weakest to Ice Water Dragon Habitat Ruins of Wyveria Size 2,058.07 1,465.34 Related Monsters Fulgur Anjanath Anjanath Generation Sixth Guardian Fulgur Anjanath is a Construct based on Fulgur Anjanath , first introduced in Monster Hunter Wilds . Contents 1 Physiology 2 Behavior and Abilities 3 Game Appearances 3.1 Main Series 4 In-Game Description 5 Notes Physiology [ ] Guardian Fulgur Anjanath have white patterns on their head and body, glowing veins covering their head and body, and entirely black eyes. Behavior and Abilities [ ] Like other Guardians, Guardian Fulgur Anjanath can harness the power of Wyvern Milk to enhance and sustain themselves, gaining greater power. Their biting attacks release powerful explosions that create Wylkrystals if they come into contact with pools of Wyvern Milk, which they can then detonate using Thunder attacks. Their wounds regenerate if not quickly destroyed. Guardian Fulgur Anjanath is an extremely aggressive monster that will attack anything it comes into contact with. Game Appearances [ ] Click on Expand to see contents. Main Series [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds (Introduced) Monster Hunter Wilds: Ascendance In-Game Description [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds An artificial monster species created by an ancient civilization and imbued with Fulgur Anjanath's power. Guardian Fulgur Anjanath cannot reproduce and do not require food, relying instead on Wyvern Milk for energy. Not only can the lightning charged in their bodies be released as an attack, they can also use it to empower themselves and perform vicious biting attakcs. Notes [ ] General General Notes Repeatedly destroying Guardian Fulgur Anjanath's wounds before they can regenerate will topple it and deplete its power, rendering it temporarily unable to use Thunder attacks, cause explosions, or regenerate wounds. The next time it changes areas, it will absorb energy, leaving itself vulnerable for an extended period of time. Guardian Fulgur Anjanath has a Turf War with Xu Wu , Guardian Doshaguma and Guardian Rathalos . Alongside Guardian Ebony Odogaron , Guardian Fulgur Anjanath are the only Construct monsters to be based on Subspecies . They are also the first subspecies to appear without their base counterpart, albeit as a Guardian. ▷ Monsters appearing in Monster Hunter Wilds ( edit ) Herbivore &#160; Ceratonoth &#160; Dalthydon # Guardian Rathalos

Guardian Rathalos 護竜リオレウス &#160; (Gādian Rioreusu) Carves · Equipment · Gallery · Videos Ecology English Title Guardian Fire Wyvern Japanese Title 護火竜 Chinese Name 护火龙 (Simplified) 護火龍 (Traditional) Korean Name 수호룡 리오레우스 Korean Title 호화룡 General Information Monster Class Construct Elements Fire Ailments Fireblight Poison Weakest to Thunder Dragon Habitat Ruins of Wyveria Size 2,130.27 1,533.79 Related Monsters Rathalos Generation Sixth Guardian Rathalos is a Construct based on Rathalos , first introduced in Monster Hunter Wilds . Contents 1 Physiology 2 Behavior and Abilities 3 Game Appearances 3.1 Main Series 4 In-Game Description 5 Notes Physiology [ ] Guardian Rathalos have distinctive glowing veins over their head, body, wings, and legs in addition to white patterns on their wings and pale red scales. They have solid black eyes. Behavior and Abilities [ ] Like other Guardians, Guardian Rathalos can harness the power of Wyvern Milk to enhance and sustain themselves, gaining greater power. Their claw attacks release powerful explosions that create Wylkrystals if they come into contact with pools of Wyvern Milk, which they can then detonate using Fire attacks. Their wounds regenerate if not quickly destroyed. Game Appearances [ ] Click on Expand to see contents. Main Series [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds (Introduced) Monster Hunter Wilds: Ascendance In-Game Description [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds An artifical monster species created by an ancient civilization and imbued with Rathalos's power. Guardian Rathalos cannot reproduce and do not require food, relying instead on Wyvern Milk for energy. Just like the original Rathalos, they are superior fliers and will attack more violently with their claws while airborne. Notes [ ] General General Notes Guardian Rathalos was the very first Construct monster be revealed other than the flagship monster Guardian Arkveld . Repeatedly destroying Guardian Rathalos's wounds before they can regenerate will topple it and deplete its power, rendering it temporarily unable to cause explosions or regenerate wounds. The next time it changes areas, it will absorb energy, leaving itself vulnerable for an extended period of time. Guardian Rathalos has a Turf War with Xu Wu , Guardian Fulgur Anjanath and Quematrice . It has the standard area battle theme during its fight rather than the new Rathalos theme the base monster recieved. Its roar is slightly different from the original Rathalos, being more echoey and similar to the Apex's roar effect. Despite its name, it has nothing to do with Guardian Ratha from Monster Hunter Stories 2: Wings of Ruin . ▷ Monsters appearing in Monster Hunter Wilds ( edit ) Herbivore &#160; Ceratonoth &#160; # Guardian Seikret

Guardian Seikret 護竜セクレト &#160; (Gādian Sekureto) Gallery • Ecology • Videos English Name Guardian Seikret English Title None Japanese Title None Chinese Name 护鹭鹰龙 (Simplified) 護鷺鷹龍 (Traditional) Korean Name 수호룡 세크레트 General Information Monster Class Construct Size Small Related Monsters Seikret Generation Sixth Guardian Seikrets are Constructs introduced in Monster Hunter Wilds . Contents 1 Appearance 2 Behavior and Abilities 3 Game Appearances 3.1 Main Series 4 In-Game Descriptions 5 Notes Appearance [ ] Guardian Seikrets resemble their natural counterparts, albeit with the grey and white color scheme common to other Constructs. They possess sharp claws on their forelegs. The feathers on their heads and backs are more spike-like. Behavior and Abilities [ ] Unlike normal Seikrets , Guardian Seikrets are aggressive and will attack hunters on sight. They will generally use their teeth and claws to inflict damage, but they also have a Guardian energy powered leap attack similar to their larger Guardian counterparts. They generally travel in packs of two to four but may call for assistance if they believe they are in danger. They're mostly active during Wyveria's Inclemency. Game Appearances [ ] Click on Expand to see contents. Main Series [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds (Introduced) In-Game Descriptions [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds Artificial wyverns created by an ancient civilization. They dash through the Ruins of Wyveria in search of Wyvern Milk, their source of energy. Unlike the original Seikret, the Guardian types are aggressive and have sharp, dangerous claws on their unique forelegs. They generally travel in groups of two or three, but if threatened, they may call for more help and try to surround the adversary. Notes [ ] General General Notes Guardian Seikrets are the only Construct to be a small monster. They are also the only Construct that can be awakened before the Inclemency with a slinger. ▷ Monsters appearing in Monster Hunter Wilds ( edit ) Herbivore &#160; Ceratonoth &#160; Dalthydon &#160; # Hirabami

Hirabami ヒラバミ &#160; (Hirabami) Carves · Equipment · Gallery · Videos Ecology English Title Wind Shear Wyvern Japanese Title 風鋏竜 Chinese Name 风铗龙 (Simplified) 風鋏龍 (Traditional) Korean Name 히라바미 Korean Title 풍협룡 General Information Monster Class Leviathan Elements Ice Ailments Frostblight Weakest to Fire Thunder Habitat Iceshard Cliffs Rimechain Peak Ruins of Wyveria Wounded Hollow Size 1,908.24 1,476.81 Generation Sixth Hirabami is a Leviathan first introduced in Monster Hunter Wilds . Contents 1 Physiology 2 Behavior and Abilities 3 Game Appearances 3.1 Main Series 4 In-Game Description 5 Notes Physiology [ ] Hirabami is a leviathan with a peculiar body shape, similar to that of Somnacanth . Its body is mainly colored a dark purple, with shades of cyan all throughout. Its head is pointy and covered in segmented plates, while its lower jaw can split in two. The inside of its mouth is brightly-colored, and it has two small white eyes that lack pupils. A large membrane begins from the sides of its head, running down the entirety of its long neck and ending at its shoulders. Each of its arms has a fin running from the elbow to the hand, and is tipped with a single, sickle-shaped claw. Its back legs are small and thin, ending in flat pads. Its tail is thin at the base but thicker at the end, and culminates in eleven pairs of clamping claws. Behavior and Abilities [ ] Hirabami is noteworthy for its ability to levitate on the air without the need for wings, similar to Ibushi and Narwa , using the wind-catching membrane on its neck. It can also use the appendages at the end of its tail to hang from rock arches and cave ceilings. Finally, it can spit an icy projectile from its mouth that can freeze the hunter in place. They can be seen and fought in trios. Game Appearances [ ] Click on Expand to see contents. Main Series [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds (Introduced) In-Game Description [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds Leviathans that inhabit the Iceshard Cliffs. They have sharp, pincer like tails and peculiar membranes on their necks that catch the wind and keep them aloft. Hirabami can sometimes be sighted nesting in groups along the cliffs, using their scales or foreclaws to cling to walls or ceilings while they sleep. Notes [ ] General General Notes Hirabami has a turf war with Blangonga . A pack of Hirabami appear and lie sleeping in the Rimechain Peak 's second area. They can be awoken by the player by firing a Torch Pod at one to distract the areas ruler, Jin Dahaad . Its tail can be broken the most times out of any individual part of any monster in the series, being able to be broken five times. ▷ Monsters appearing in Monster Hunter Wilds ( edit ) Herbivore # Jin Dahaad

Jin Dahaad ジン・ダハド &#160; (Jin Dahado) Carves · Equipment · Gallery · Videos Ecology English Title Frozen Peak Wyvern Lord of Ice Japanese Title 凍峰竜 霜烈なる氷塞の主 Chinese Name 冻峰龙 (Simplified) 凍峰龍 (Traditional) Chinese Title 霜裂的冰塞之主 (Simplified) Korean Name 진 다하드 Korean Title 동봉룡 서리를 찢는 얼음요새의 주인 General Information Monster Class Leviathan Elements Ice Ailments Iceblight Frostblight Weakest to Fire Habitat Iceshard Cliffs Rimechain Peak Size 4,560.89 Related Monsters None Generation Sixth Jin Dahaad is a Leviathan first introduced in Monster Hunter Wilds . Contents 1 Physiology 2 Behavior and Abilities 3 Game Appearances 3.1 Main Series 4 In-Game Description 5 Notes Physiology [ ] Jin Dahaad is an enormous monster with a long, serpentine body and four limbs. Its head is small, with small grey eyes mostly obscured beneath metalic facial plates, and a bottom jaw capable of splitting in two, as well as several sets of metallic, jagged horns forming a "hood" near where the head meets the neck. Its neck is long, surrounded on either side by metallic plates similar in shape to ribs and vertebrae. A row of upward-pointing, rod-shaped plates grow on top of the monster's shoulders, while another row grows horizontally across them. A similar arrangement is also present on its hips. Its chest has several large spikes protruding outwards, and the outline of its ribcage is seemingly visible. Its limbs continue the pattern of plates seen on the neck, with the forelimbs specifically having an extra row of spikes above the wrists, and ending in angular claws. Its tail is long and sinuous, featuring rod-like plates like on the shoulders and neck across its length, and ending in an arrangement of spines pointing in various directions. Behavior and Abilities [ ] The Jin Dahaad is the powerful apex predator of the Iceshard Cliffs , and becomes most active during the region's Inclemency, the Frostwinds. The titanic monster uses its own enormous body as a living heat sink , shunting heat to the coppery metallic plates on its head, neck, chest, limbs, and tail to drastically lower both its internal body temperature and that of the surroundings. It can use this technique in a variety of ways, such as to effortlessly climb on vertical surfaces, despite its enormous size, by super-cooling its limbs and generating ice on its claws to stick to surfaces, allowing it to fight on cliffsides. By using the plates on its head, it can fire a powerful freezing beam from its jaws that can freeze hunters and even stun other monsters, such as Hirabami . Other times, it will generate icy armor for extra protection against threats. Finally, by pushing its cooling ability to its absolute limit, it encases both itself and the entire area in a caldera of solid ice, sucking foes towards it in an icy vacuum effect (all while the leviathans' cooling plates practically burst into flame on its back), before unleashing a near-fatal explosion of snow and steam. Game Appearances [ ] Click on Expand to see contents. Main Series [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds (Introduced) In-Game Description [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds A leviathan that sits at the peak of the Iceshard Cliff's ecosystem. It surrounds its massive form in cold temperatures via ridge-like cooling organs across its body; the frost its limbs produce doubles as a potent weapon and a way to travel along walls and floating rocks. Notes [ ] General General Notes Jin Dahaad is the only Apex Monster to not have a Turf War . Jin Dahaad is first fought and repelled in Low rank; however, it has no Low rank materials. It is fought again in High rank, where it is truly slain and grants its own materials. Despite being the Apex of the Iceshard Cliffs, Jin Dahaad is not hunted here, instead being fought in the Rimechain Peak. It will however briefly appear in the Cliffs before its investigation quest becomes available and attack the hunters and monsters alike with its ice breath. If it is damaged enough during this it will retreat to the Peak, and entering the following investigation will have it start in phase 3. Jin Dahaad shares similarities to the boss fight with Zorah Magdaros , Kulve Taroth , and Safi'jiiva : Their hunts are raid-like quests. Breaking their parts, especially its plating, plays a vital role in decreasing the power and amount of ice they can produce. Breaking all of its plating renders it unable to use its ultimate attack for the rest of the hunt. Their back plating is the most important part relating to their abilities. In order to damage it, the plating on its head, pelvis, and tail must be broken first or all attacks will bounce and do minimal damage. However, it will not break until it has done its ultimate attack at least once. It has an area-wide super attack much like Safi'jiiva, and even shares the distinction of requiring the hunter to hide behind cover to not get hit. However, this attack doesn't guarantee a faint. It can be countered by weapons' counter moves, blocked by guarding (however still dealing very high damage), or lived through with the skills Guts and Moxie. Jin Dahaad can be carved 6 times and its tail can be carved twice. Jin Dahaad is currently the second largest Leviathan in the mainline series at 4,560.89 cm, beating Abyssal Lagiacrus 's 3,969.3 cm, but shorter than Nibelsnarf 's 4693.15 cm. This size is even larger than some large elder dragons. Though it is not an Elder Dragon, Jin Dahaad cannot be captured, similar to Raging Brachydios in Monster Hunter World: Iceborne. However it can be shock trapped. Unlike Raging Brachydios, which can theoretically be captured, but will result quest failure, it seems Jin Dahaad cannot be captured at any circumstances. Arch-Tempered Jin Dahaad was added in Title Update 4, and made available through an event quest launched December 23, 2025. Several of its attacks are doubled up, and it gains a 360 degree tail swing and wide arc ice breath attack. Its ice breath and ice gas causes exploding icicles to appear on the ground. ▷ Monsters appearing in Monster Hunter Wilds ( edit ) Herbivore &#160; Ceratonoth &#160; Dalthydon &#160; Rafma Neopteron # Lala Barina

Lala Barina ラバラ・バリナ &#160; (Rabara Barina) Carves · Equipment · Gallery · Videos Ecology English Title Thorny Flower Spider Japanese Title 刺花蜘蛛 Chinese Name 刺花蜘蛛 (Simplified & Traditional) Korean Name 라바라 바리나 Korean Title 자화지주 General Information Monster Class Temnoceran Ailments Paralysis Weakest to Fire Habitat Scarlet Forest Ruins of Wyveria Wounded Hollow Size 866.44 621.88 Generation Sixth “ A Temnoceran that resides in the Scarlet Forest. Its thorax, which can be opened at will, resembles a rose in bloom. „ Lala Barina is a Temnoceran first introduced in Monster Hunter Wilds . Contents 1 Physiology 2 Behavior and Abilities 3 Game Appearances 3.1 Main Series 4 In-Game Description 5 Notes Physiology [ ] Lala Barina is a large temnoceran with a unique appearance. Its six legs are jointed and are covered in sharp-looking spikes. Its thorax and abdomen are instead coated in fluffy hairs. Its abdomen culminates in a retractable stinger, which it can use to sting enemies. The frontmost pair of legs is normally tucked within its body, but can also open up to reveal appendages that resemble clawed hands. Its head is small, with two large, orange eyes, two slightly smaller pairs of eyes above, a cluster of three small eyes in the center of the forehead and a pair of chelicerae. Its most distinguishing feature, however, is the fact that, when enraged, part of its abdomen opens to reveal a tuft of red hairs shaped like a rose bloom. Behavior and Abilities [ ] Lala Barina can spin a scarlet, glowing silk from their mouths, which they can use for attacking and binding prey or locomotion. They also use it to construct massive scarlet colored nests within the Scarlet Forest. Around these nests can be found "florets" of silk, which indicate its presence. Unlike many other monsters, Lala Barina acts in short but quick movements, and will often pause while considering its next move. In battle it uses its claws, spiked legs, stinger and mandibles to stab and slash opponents with, it can also shoot flower-like urticating hairs from its abdomen at its foes to inflict Paralysis. Game Appearances [ ] Click on Expand to see contents. Main Series [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds (Introduced) In-Game Description [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds Temnocerans native to the Scarlet Forest with distinctive, pirouette-like movements. Their fluids contain a neuroplegic agent which they mix with water to produce red, petal-like bristles on their abdomens. When agitated, Lala Barina open these bristles like a deadly, blossoming flower and release them as florets that paralyze whatever they come into contact with. Notes [ ] General General Notes The name Lala Barina is a direct transliteration from the Japanese name, ラバラ・バリナ (Rabara Barina), which likely derives from laba-laba , Indonesian for "spider", bara (薔薇), Japanese for "rose", and Spanish La bailarina ("the ballerina"). It is the first tempered monster encountered, appearing alongside Guardian Fulgur Anjanath. ▷ Monsters appearing in Monster Hunter Wilds ( edit ) Herbivore &#160; Ceratonoth &#160; Dalthydon &#160; # Nerscylla

Nerscylla ネルスキュラ &#160; (Nerusukyura) Carves · Equipment · Gallery · Videos Ecology English Title Shadow Spider Japanese Title 影蜘蛛 Chinese Name 影蜘蛛 (Simplified & Traditional) Korean Name 네르스큐라 Korean Title 영지주 General Information Monster Class Temnoceran Elements None Ailments Poison Noxious Poison (High and G-rank) Sleep (4U Low-rank, GU High and G-rank) Webbed Weakest to Fire Thunder Habitat Sunken Hollow Primal Forest Everwood Marshlands Oilwell Basin Iceshard Cliffs Rimechain Peak Monsonne Plains Babda Rainforest Sunpetal Plains Size 1126.57 651.63 Related Monsters Nerscylla Hatchling Shrouded Nerscylla Rakna-Kadaki Pyre Rakna-Kadaki Rachnoid Pyrantula Generation Fourth Nerscylla is a Temnoceran first introduced in Monster Hunter 4 . Contents 1 Physiology 2 Abilities 3 Habitat 4 Game Appearances 4.1 Main Series 4.2 Spin-offs 5 In-Game Description 6 MH4U Breakable Parts 7 Other Non-Subspecies Forms 7.1 Nerscylla Clone 8 Notes 9 References Physiology [ ] Nerscylla physically resembles a real life arachnid , but is much larger in size. Its body is mainly white, with dark blue and gray markings around its joints and orange hairs on its appendages and abdomen. Its front claws are a bright orange, whereas its other claws are a dark brown. Nerscylla is armed with four powerful legs and two claws, each one tipped with a formidable barbed hook, used for holding prey as well as allowing it to grip onto many types of surfaces. At the tip of its abdomen is a large stinger it may use to attack. Sharp spikes formed from crystalized poison protrude from its back, as well as barbed spikes on its legs which it uses to keep Gypceros hides draped over its back. In addition, it possesses a massive pair of retractable jaws and fangs which can inflict a venomous bite. It can produce a strong, sticky webbing that it uses to trap prey and build nests. Abilities [ ] Nerscylla is adaptable to different environments and capable of spinning a large amount of silk web very quickly. The silk it uses to swing itself across the air and to capture prey is very durable and sticky, so breaking free is difficult. Nerscylla ensnares its prey with the web, then reels them in to strike. The stinger at the tip of its abdomen contains a tranquilizing fluid that can cause the Sleep status. In higher ranks, Nerscylla loses the ability to put hunters to sleep, but gains the Deadly Poison status instead. Nerscylla found in the Forbidden Lands are known to produce their young, known as Nerscylla Hatchlings in the Iceshard Cliffs , that will swarm any hunter nearby. They also have a habit of playing with their food, as seen when one abducted Rove. Habitat [ ] Nerscylla weave nests of durable web in the Sunken Hollow . When they abandon their nest, they can be seen wandering in other areas such as the Primal Forest and the Everwood . They also appear in the arctic winds of the Iceshard Cliffs and the raging magma of the Oilwell Basin. They are also known to nest near farming land, where livestock are found, and prey on them with the occasional and unfortunate farmer. Game Appearances [ ] Click on Expand to see contents. Main Series [ ] Monster Hunter 4 (Introduced) Monster Hunter 4 Ultimate Monster Hunter Generations Ultimate Monster Hunter Wilds Spin-offs [ ] Monster Hunter Stories Monster Hunter Stories 2: Wings of Ruin Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection Monster Hunter Spirits Monster Hunter Riders In-Game Description [ ] Monster Hunter 4 別名”影蜘蛛”。縄張りに巣を張り、獲物を捕える鋏角種のモンスター。状態異常にして弱らせた獲物を粘着質の糸で膠着、鋏状の巨大な顎で捕喰する。この地方ではゲリョスの捕食が多くみられる。食べ残した獲物の皮を身にまとう習性がある Threat Level (危険度): ★★★★ Monster Hunter 4 Ultimate A Temnoceran that spins webs to defend territory and hunt prey. They bind foes that have been weakened by ailments and devour them with giant mandibles. They are fond of wearing the hides left by the Gypceros prey they consume. Threat Level (危険度): ★★★★ Monster Hunter Generations Ultimate A Temnoceran that spins webs to defend territory and hunt prey. They bind foes that have been weakened by ailments and devour them with giant mandibles. They are fond of wearing the hides left by the Gypceros prey they consume. Threat Level (危険度): ★★★★ Monster Hunter Wilds Temnocerans that inhabit the Iceshard Cliffs. They weave vast, sticky webs across their territory, using them and various status ailments to corner prey before utilizing pincer-like jaws to finish it off. Sometimes dead Gypceros can be found in their webs, the hides of which Nerscylla are known to adorn themselves with after devouring the rest of the carcass. Monster Hunter Stories Temnocerans that build nests of sticky webbing to ensnare prey, which they rend with their mandibles. Monster Hunter Stories 2: Wings of Ruin Temnocerans that build nests of sticky webbing. They rend prey with their pincer-like jaws after weakening them with abnormal statuses and ensnaring them in web. They will sometimes adorn themselves with the skin of morsels they have not completely finished eating. Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection A Temnoceran that weaves nests to defend its territory and hunt. It ensnares prey, weakening them with abnormal statuses, then feasts using its razor-sharp mandibles. MH4U Breakable Parts [ ] Hide (x1) Spikes (x2) Claw (x1) Other Non-Subspecies Forms [ ] Nerscylla Clone [ ] A clone of Nerscylla created by Omega Planetes . Notes [ ] General MH4U MHWilds General Notes Nerscylla's name is named after Nergal , the Mesopotamian god of plague and war, and Neoscona scylla , the scientific name of an orb-weaver spider. Potential name candidates for Nerscylla included Azravel and Thranid. &#91; 1 &#93; Nerscylla was voted 108th place in the Hunters' Choice for Top Monster during Monster Hunter's 20th Anniversary. &#91; 2 &#93; This is the highest placement for a Temnoceran class monster in the poll. The Gypceros hide on Nerscylla can be broken along with its claw and the poison spikes on its back twice. Its body structure is somewhat similar to Carapaceons like Daimyo Hermitaur &#160;and Shogun Ceanataur . When climbing upside down, poison will drip from Nerscylla's back. This will sometimes leave a shiny. This will not happen if its back is broken twice. If Nerscylla is infected by Frenzy Virus , frenzy drip will fall instead of poison. If Nerscylla puts a hunter to sleep and the hunter falls asleep, Nerscylla will close in on the hunter, observing to make sure the hunter is asleep. Nerscylla will then back up and then do its mandible attack on the hunter while he/she is waking up. Nerscylla's web attack&#160;can cause an Ailment Web Bound , which is similar to Muddy and Snowman, but differs by leaving the hunter stuck in place. In addition, Nerscylla will uniquely close in on the hunter and wrap the hunter up or it can use&#160;a small thin strand of web and pull the hunter closer to itself and will either attempt to sleep/poison the hunter or attack the hunter while he/she is recovering. It can also wrap the hunter and their Palicoes in webbing if they get stuck in some of the balls of webbing on the ground, and will then proceed to drag them up into the top part of the area. When weak Nerscylla will sleep upside down hanging on a web, making it a perfect time to sleep-bomb and break its back. Gypceros &#160;is the favored prey of Nerscylla, and it wears the Bird Wyvern 's hide on its back and other parts of its body. It kills Gypceros to gain their rubbery hides, and will also replace the skin when needed or after molting. In the Sunken Hollow 's area 5 there are&#160;multiple web-covered&#160;Gypceros corpses hanging from the ceiling, confirming that&#160;Nerscylla preys on Gypceros. When low on stamina, its web shot attack will fail. It will also trip and fall on its back after every attempt at launching itself with its web. It will feed on carcasses to regain stamina. Sometimes when enraged, it will perform a "Fake Taunt", in which it will rub its claws, then instantly turn at the hunter and bite with its extended jaws. Monster Hunter 4 Ultimate Nerscylla first appears in 4-star Village Quests. It can be infected by the Frenzy Virus . In High-rank, Nerscylla loses its ability to inflict sleep on hunters but its poison works twice as fast. In G-rank, After it extends its mandibles, it can walk forward before it does its mandible attack. Monster Hunter Wilds It has a Turf War with Gypceros where it attempts to consume the Bird Wyvern. In Wilds, Nerscylla now has a juvenile form, known as Nerscylla Hatchlings , that appear in the Iceshard Cliffs . They mostly serve to distract the hunter in great numbers, in addition to attacking them. It was redesigned after its movie version. Title Update 3's Final Fantasy XIV collaboration introduced the Nerscylla Clone, a copy created by Omega Planetes that is able to fire lasers from its mouth and projectiles from its back alike the otherworldly monster itself. Unlike the crystal carrying Kulu-Ya-Ku from the collaboration in Monster Hunter: World which is a one-time only assignment, the Nerscylla Clone can be fought again in its own quest. The Nerscylla Clone appears during the actual Omega Planetes hunt, where it is summoned as Omega charges its Delta Attack. Hunters must slay the clone before the attack is fully charged to create a shield or they will be one-shot. # Nu Udra

Nu Udra ヌ・エグドラ &#160; (Nu Egudora) Carves · Equipment · Gallery · Videos Ecology English Title Hell Flame Octopus The Black Flame Ebon Hellfire Japanese Title 獄焔蛸 黒い炎 黒き獄炎 Chinese Name 狱焰蛸 (Simplified) 獄焰鱆 (Traditional) Chinese Title 黑炎 (Simplified & Traditional) 黑色狱炎 (Simplified) 黑色獄炎 (Traditional) Korean Name 누 이그드라 Korean Title 옥염소 검은 불꽃 General Information Monster Class Cephalopod Elements Fire Ailments Fireblight Weakest to Water Habitat Oilwell Basin Size 2,386.81 1,884.32 Generation Sixth “ This mysterious monster is known to the people of Azuz as the Black Flame. „ Nu Udra is a Cephalopod introduced in Monster Hunter Wilds . Contents 1 Physiology 2 Behavior and Abilities 3 In-Game Description 4 Notes Physiology [ ] Nu Udra is a massive monster heavily resembling an octopus. It has six large limbs which it uses for locomotion and attacking, with its remaining two limbs being reduced and used to shield its mouth. Its dark green skin is covered in blue and black stripes, but at most times appears dark due to the creature being covered in a thick layer of black oilsilt at all times, which can be ignited at will when Nu Udra is enraged. The tentacles and mantle of the monster are covered in many thorny spines. Its most striking features are its bioluminescent blue eye stalks, along with glowing spots on the end of it's main six limbs; these glowing spots act as sensory organs as the cephalopod itself is mostly blind. Similar to Xu Wu, it has offspring that hatch en masse known as Nu Yaya. Unlike Xu Wu's offspring however, they only seem to be present for a brief period of time, shortly after the end of the Firespring. Behavior and Abilities [ ] Much about Nu Udra's ecology is a mystery, although it is known to be most active during the " Firespring " inclemency of the Oilwell Basin . As the apex predator of the Oilwell Basin, it is big and powerful enough to subdue and defeat powerful monsters like Ajarakan , able to hold three with its tentacles at once; it employs this grappling attack to pull hunters towards it so it can finish them off with fire from its siphon. Nu Udra mainly uses its tentacles to sense it's surroundings, as well as weapons to whip and smash enemies. It can additionally attack directly behind with its tentacles without needing to turn to face the hunter. Like real life octopuses, Nu Udra can burrow into the ground to ambush opponents and squeeze through tight spaces to escape from enemies. When enraged it will open its eyes, its face glows bright orange and it will coat its whole body in fire, allowing it to launch oil fireballs, create heat waves, and whip opponents with its flaming tentacles. Nu Udra can spew large amounts of fire from its siphon to burn opponents from a distance. Its most powerful attack unleashes a furious rain of burning oil in all directions, however this attack extinguishes its fire, exhausts it, and forces it back to its base mode. In-Game Description [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds The cephalopod that reigns supreme over Oilwell Basin's ecosystem. Usually when sighted, Nu Udra is covered in oilsilt, but when enraged it will ignite the coating, transfiguring itself into a horror of flame. The oculi that appear on its body and many arms are presumed to be sensory organs, but the truth is still being investigated. Threat Level (危険度): ★-★★★★★ Notes [ ] General General Notes Nu Udra is the first cephalopod type monster to not be classified as an Elder Dragon . As Nu Udra primarily relies on sensory organs in its tentacles for detection instead of eye vision, it is immune to Flash Bombs. Nu Udra has a turf war with Ajarakan . Each of Nu Udra's tentacles can be cut and will continue to wriggle as if still alive until they rot, at which point they are no longer carve-able. Tempered and Arch Tempered Nu Udra will always start out enraged and will remain so until it is knocked out of it. Despite not being tempered this is also the case for the Nu Udra encountered during the High Rank part of the main story. Arch Tempered Nu Udra was added into the game via the third title update and became available on October 22nd, 2025 (UTC). It is the third non-Elder Dragon to be Arch-Tempered and the first Arch Tempered in Wilds to have a 9★ quest level. It gains numerous new attacks, a large speed increase, and being near it in its enraged state will deal continuous damage. Noticeably, the eyes of Arch Tempered individuals glow purple instead of the usual blue. The names of Nu Udra's weapons come from the word for octopus in different languages: Greatsword: Krake - German Hammer: Pulpo - Spanish Lance: Osminog (осьминог) - Russian Charge Blade: Pweza - Swahili Switch Axe: Zhangyu (章鱼) - Mandarin Chinese Light Bowgun: Chobotnice - Czech Heavy Bowgun: Muneo (문어) - Korean ▷ Monsters appearing in Monster Hunter Wilds ( edit ) Herbivore &#160; Ceratonoth # Quematrice

Quematrice ケマトリス &#160; (Kematorisu) Carves · Equipment · Gallery · Videos Ecology English Title Flame Tail Wyvern Japanese Title 炎尾竜 Chinese Name 炎尾龙 (Simplified) 炎尾龍 (Traditional) Korean Name 케마트리스 Korean Title 염미룡 General Information Monster Class Brute Wyvern Elements Fire Ailments Fireblight Weakest to Water Habitat Windward Plains Ruins of Wyveria Wounded Hollow Size 1,542.76 1,107.30 Generation Sixth “ Brute wyverns with disproportionately long tails. They spread a flammable substance, then ignite it by dragging their tails along the ground. „ Quematrice is a Brute Wyvern first introduced in Monster Hunter Wilds . Contents 1 Physiology 2 Behavior and Abilities 3 Game Appearances 3.1 Main Series 4 In-Game Description 5 Notes Physiology [ ] Quematrice is a Brute Wyvern covered in mainly green scales with red and blue stripes, like most brute wyverns it has short arms and large hind legs used for locomotion. It has reddish orange crests around its eyes that gives the appearance of a mask, a large comb atop its head, and a wattle on its throat, giving it the appearance of a cockatrice. It has an unsually large tail draped in layers of loose skin, from which it emits a highly flammable oil that can ignite with friction. Behavior and Abilities [ ] Quematrice's most defining trait is the flammable oil it emits from its tail; this oil, when struck, ignites both the environment and anything unlucky enough to be too close. Quematrice can sweep its tail across the ground to create large waves of fire to burn its enemies. It is known for being rather aggressive, and has a tendency to fight smaller monsters over carrion. Game Appearances [ ] Click on Expand to see contents. Main Series [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds (Introduced) In-Game Description [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds Brute wyverns with disproportionately long tails. They spread a flammable substance which vaporizes quickly, then ignite it by dragging their tails along the ground. By nature, Quematrice rarely hunt their own prey, and instead scavenge herbivore carrion left behind by other predators. This often leads to confrontations with other, smaller carnivores. Notes [ ] General General Notes The name Quematrice is a combination of quemar , meaning "to burn" in Spanish, and cockatrice , a legendary, serpentine monster, often depicted with the head and wings of a rooster and the body of a serpent. ▷ Monsters appearing in Monster Hunter Wilds ( edit ) Herbivore &#160; Ceratonoth &#160; Dalthydon &#160; Rafma Neopteron &#160; Bulaqchi &#160; Comaqchi &#160;
