# Rathalos

Rathalos リオレウス &#160; (Rioreusu) Carves · Equipment · Gallery · Videos Ecology English Title Fire Wyvern King of the Skies Japanese Title 火竜 天空の王者 Chinese Name 雄火龙 (Simplified) 雄火龍 (Traditional) 火龙 (MHRise; Simplified) 火龍 (MHRise; Traditional) Chinese Title 天空王者 (Simplified) 天空王者 (Traditional) Korean Name 리오레우스 Korean Title 화룡 General Information Monster Class Flying Wyvern Elements Fire Dragon ( 1st Gen ? , 2nd Gen ) Ailments Fireblight ( 3rd Gen onwards) Stun Poison Weakest to Ice ( 2nd Gen ) Water ( 2nd Gen ) Dragon ( 2nd Gen onwards) Thunder ( 3rd Gen onwards) Habitat Forest and Hills Old Swamp Old Volcano Volcano Tower Tower 3 Deserted Island Volcano (3rd) Ancestral Steppe Volcanic Hollow Heaven's Mount Tower Summit Everwood Jurassic Frontier Verdant Hills Ruined Pinnacle Ancient Forest Elder's Recess Guiding Lands Shrine Ruins Lava Caverns Red Stronghold Infernal Springs Citadel Forlorn Arena (MHRS) Scarlet Forest Oilwell Basin Ruins of Wyveria Gorge Highland Interceptor's Base Fortress Ruins Esther Lake Moonlands Ghost Rune Volcanoes Firefly Mountain Stream Pondry Hills Sacrosanctum/Lazlion Size 2248.6 1140.6 Related Monsters Azure Rathalos Silver Rathalos Dreadking Rathalos Apex Rathalos Guardian Rathalos Zenith Rathalos Flame Rathalos Destruction Wyvern Rathalos Zerureusu Razewing Ratha Rathian Pink Rathian Gold Rathian Dreadqueen Rathian Apex Rathian Conflagration Rathian Scorching Heat Rathian Skyscale Rathalos Generation First “ Terrible wyverns called "Kings of the Skies". Along with Rathians , they stake wide territories centered around their nests. Rathalos descend on invaders from the sky, attacking with poison claws and breath of fire. „ - MHGU Description Rathalos are male Flying Wyverns introduced in Monster Hunter . Having appeared in every single Monster Hunter game in the series, including spin-offs, Rathalos are considered one of the greatest icon of the Monster Hunter series . Contents 1 Physiology 2 Abilities 3 Behavior 4 Habitat 5 Game Appearances 5.1 Main Series 5.2 Frontier 5.3 Spin-offs 5.4 Crossovers 6 Game Data 6.1 MHP3rd Element/Status Effectiveness 6.2 MH3 Damage Effectiveness 6.3 MHW Damage Effectiveness 6.4 Item Effectiveness 6.5 MH4U Breakable Parts 7 Materials 7.1 Monster Hunter 4 Ultimate 8 Equipment 9 In-Game Description 10 Soundtrack 11 Gallery 12 Notes Physiology [ ] Rathalos are large, bipedal wyverns with a spiny, armored hide covering their body. Their outer shell features much brighter and more vibrant colors than that of their female counterpart, the Rathian . It is primarily bright red, with black markings throughout. Like the Rathian, Rathalos possess a flame sac which is used to produce deadly flaming projectiles from the mouth. The talons upon their feet are highly poisonous and are known to inflict toxic mortal wounds on larger prey. In addition, their long, thick tail features a heavy spiked club at the end. Rathalos' wing membranes feature ornate patterns which are likely used to attract potential mates. Abilities [ ] Rathalos are expert flyers, and as such are prone to hunting from the skies. By launching a surprise aerial attack, they can inflict poisonous wounds with their talons or burn prey with flaming projectiles. On the ground, Rathalos remain formidable opponents. Using their powerful legs, they can chase down prey from a distance or inflict blunt-force damage with their clubbed tail. At a close enough range, they are known to use their sharp teeth to bite at foes as well. Some Rathalos are capable of staying in the air and launching a "few" fireballs before landing back to the ground. Behavior [ ] Rathalos are highly territorial monsters, and as such, are aggressive towards intruders. They will chase away or attempt to kill any monster which may pose a threat to their land. While hunting, Rathalos will stalk prey from the air before swooping in for the kill. Once they have successfully brought down their prey, they will carry it away to eat in private, safe from scavengers or other large monsters which might attempt to steal the kill. Habitat [ ] Rathalos have been spotted in many kinds of environments, from the temperate Forest and Hills to the extreme heat of the Volcano . As a master of flight, they will travel far and wide, searching for prey from the skies. Rathalos is also the apex predator of the Ancient Forest alongside Nargacuga . Its claws are filled with a poison that weakens its quarry. Monster Hunter 3 Terrible wyverns called the 'Kings of the Skies'. Together with Rathian , they stake wide territories centered around their nests. Rathalos descend on invaders from the sky, attacking with poison claws and breath of fire. Monster Hunter Portable 3rd 天空の王者と呼称され、畏れられる飛竜。雌火竜と共に巣を中心とした広いテリトリーを形成し、空から監視する。テリトリーに侵入した者には優れた飛行能力で上空から急降下し、毒を含む強力な爪で襲い掛かってくる。火を吐くことから “火竜” と呼ばれる。 Monster Hunter 3 Ultimate Terrible wyverns called the 'Kings of the Skies'. Together with Rathian , they stake wide territories centered around their nests. Rathalos descend on invaders from the sky, attacking with poison claws and breath of fire. Monster Hunter 4 天空の王者と呼称され、畏れられる飛竜。雌火竜と共に巣を中心とした広いテリトリーを形成し、空から監視する。 テリトリーに侵入した者には優れた飛行能力で上空から急降下し、毒を含む強力な爪で襲い掛かってくる。 火を吐くことから”火竜”と呼ばれる。 Threat Level (危険度): ★★★★★ Monster Hunter 4 Ultimate Terrible wyverns called the 'Kings of the Skies'. Along with Rathians, they stake wide territories centered around their nests. Rathalos descend on invaders from the sky, attacking with poison claws and breath of fire. Threat Level (危険度): ★★★★★ Monster Hunter Generations Terrible wyverns called "Kings of the Skies". Along with Rathians, they stake wide territories centered around their nests. Rathalos descend on invaders from the sky, attacking with poison claws and breath of fire. Threat Level (危険度): ★★★★★ Monster Hunter World The apex monster of the Ancient Forest, also known as the "King of the Skies." A terrible wyvern that descends upon invaders, attacking with poison claws and fiery breath. Monster Hunter Rise Terrible wyverns called "Kings of the Skies." Along with Rathian, they stake wide territories centered around their nest. Rathalos descend on invaders from the sky, attacking with poison claws and a breath of fire. Threat Level (危険度): ★★★★★★ Monster Hunter Wilds Fearsome male wyverns known as the "Kings of the Skies." Alongside the Rathian, they stake out a wide territory around their nests, and it is the male's role to watch from above. Armed with powerful poisonous claws and fiery breath, Rathalos use their aerial prowess to descend on invaders. Monster Hunter Stories Dubbed the King of the Skies, it dominates battle with its powerful wings and poison-tipped claws. Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection Simply mentioning Flying Wyverns brings this fire-breathing monster to mind. Aptly known as the "King of the Skies," it spends little time on land, instead using its powerful wings to hunt from above. Monster Hunter Now A fearsome flying wyvern who has earned the moniker "King of the Skies." Together with its female counterpart, it forms territory around their nest, keeping close watch from above. With its powerful wings, it swiftly descends to intercept any trespassers, assailing them with its mighty, poisonous claws. In addition, it is well known for its ability to breathe fire. Monster Hunter Frontier G (?) Monster Hunter Online 分类 飞龙种。 生境范围 栖息范围极广，主要为森丘和火山区域。 生态位 雄火龙是裹覆着暗红色鳞片的雄性飞龙，相比雌火龙极强的陆战能力，雄火龙更擅长在天空中飞翔，藉此可以将狩猎的范围拓展到更加广阔的领域。由于其强大的飞行能力，几乎统御着整片天空，因此也被人称为“空之王者”。 适应性 和雌火龙一样，雄火龙同样拥有喷吐灼热火焰以及强大的破坏力，坚硬的鳞甲能够有效的保护自身，抵挡来自外界的攻击。 行为 结合空中飞行的能力，雄火龙的机动性更上一个层次，因此他也将给猎人带来更大的威胁。由于长时间在空中搜寻猎物，雄火龙的洞察力也更加敏锐，总是能找到猎物没有防备的时候给予猎物致命的一击。所以，当雄火龙在空中盘旋的时候，也请千万不要掉以轻心。 Soundtrack [ ] https://monsterhunter.fandom.com/wiki/File:RoarMHWilds.mp3 Loops from 0:15 BPM: 140 (4/4 > 11/4 > 4/4 > 3/4 > 4/4) Remix of the Forest and Hills theme. Exclusive to Monster Hunter Wilds. Gallery [ ] For more images, see Rathalos Photo Gallery Notes [ ] General MH3 MH4U MHW MHRise MHWilds Frontier MHO MHNow MHST3 General Notes Rathalos is the flagship monster of the original Monster Hunter game, and is commonly considered as the flagship monster of the entire series. So far, Rathalos have appeared in every single Monster Hunter game in the series, even including the spin-off MH Diary: Poka Poka Felyne Village . Rathalos has made crossover appearances as a boss in other video game titles such as Metal Gear Solid: Peace Walker , Final Fantasy XIV , and Super Smash Bros. Ultimate ; and in a playable capacity in mobile titles TEPPEN , Dragalia Lost , Puzzle & Dragons , and Arknights . According to one of the Uniqlo Monster Hunter 10th Anniversary T-Shirts , the size of the foot of a Rathalos is 221 centimeters. This may also apply to Azure Rathalos , as the shirt is focused on the main monsters of every generation. Rathalos may use his dive bomb attack as the very first attack during a hunt (though this happens quite rarely). Rathalos's roar requires Earplugs to block. Rathalos is one of the four monsters that have made an appearance in every game in the series. The others are Rathian and Felyne . Before Wilds, Rathalos, Lagiacrus , and Estrellian were the only flagship monsters that didn't have an exclusive battle theme, however Rathalos is mostly associated with the Forest and Hills battle theme as it was designed with the wyvern in mind. The only exception of this is Monster Hunter Online , where Forest and Hills plays no matter where he is fought. This holds true for Azure Rathalos and Silver Rathalos in the same game. Some cutscenes featuring Rathalos, such as in Monster Hunter World , use leitmotifs of Forest and Hills. Rathalos gained its own battle theme in Monster Hunter Wilds , being an extended remix of Forest and Hills. Rathalos was voted 21st place in the Hunters' Choice for Top Monster during Monster Hunter's 20th Anniversary. &#91; 1 &#93; For First Generation , Second Generation , and Frontier Generation , Rathalos, Rathian, and all subspecies and forms have a more elongated body from their original Monster Hunter design. Their designs were changed to the modern body structure in Monster Hunter 3 . Additionally, Rathalos, Rathian, and their other species gain a new sound set starting in Monster Hunter: World . The only post-World game to use their old sounds is Monster Hunter Stories 2: Wings of Ruin . They gain another new sound set and roar in Monster Hunter Wilds , in addition to having both Old World and New World sounds. New related species and forms in Monster Hunter Frontier like Zerureusu and Zenith Rathalos are exempt from this, even when they released after Monster Hunter 4 . Rathalos is one of the few monsters to have more than one rival, notable Lagiacrus, Seregios, and Astalos. Monster Hunter 3 Rathalos can recover Stamina by eating a medium-sized herbivore, mainly Aptonoth , Slagtoth and Gargwa . It can pin down a hunter and restore lost Stamina, this can be stopped by running and tripping. It is weakest to the Dragon Element , followed by the Thunder Element . Breaking the wing(s) does not stop its aerial attacks or travel. Monster Hunter 4 Ultimate Rathalos&#160;gains some new moves. During Rathalos's dive bomb attack, he will now breathe fireballs during that attack, so the hunter can not stand still and heal or sharpen like in generations before. In G rank it can do Azure Rathalos's claw attacks. It can be infected by the Frenzy Virus . Its back can be broken. Monster Hunter: World Rathalos is the apex monster of the Ancient Forest . Rathalos engages in Turf Wars with Anjanath , Deviljho , Savage Deviljho , Rajang , Brachydios , Tigrex , Brute Tigrex , Odogaron , Ebony Odogaron , Glavenus , Acidic Glavenus , Zinogre , Stygian Zinogre and Nargacuga . Both Rathalos and Rathian have the ability to call for the other’s help if both are in the same locale. A different louder roar is heard when this happens. This holds true for both Sub species and Rare Species . The only other monsters capable of calling a nearby partner are Legiana and Shrieking Legiana , whose call roars are different as well. Breaking Rathalos's wings can now cause it to fail to take flight. A giant Rathalos was fought in the no longer accessible event quest for the Monster Hunter (2020) movie. The Forest and Hills battle theme played for the fight, as Rathalos is generally associated with the theme. Monster Hunter Rise In addition to the returning monsters from World, Rathalos has Turf Wars with Bishaten , Great Wroggi , Kulu-Ya-Ku , Tobi-Kadachi and Magnamalo . Rathalos gains a new attack in the air where it breathes a stream of fire from behind it before sending it towards a hunter. Rathalos gains a new attack where it tries to swipe at hunters with its talons before shooting a fireball at the target from behind. Rathalos no longer goes airborne and breathes a fireball after becoming enraged. Rathalos can appear in The Rampage . It is considered a Targetter type. In Master Rank, Rathalos gains a new attack where it can spew a stream of fire from its mouth to one of its sides while flying. It will immediately perform a claw attack afterwards. If Rathalos ends up too far after the fire stream, it will fire a fireball instead. Rathalos is excellent at anti-air defense, as it can elevate itself to however high its target is and shoot fireballs at it, or aim up and shoot fireballs on the ground. Monster Hunter Wilds For unknown reasons, Rathalos's head and back are unbreakable. Rathalos gains numerous new attacks, including the ability to fire three fireballs in a row on the ground like Rathian, only its version has it reposition by jumping after every blast. By damaging Rathalos in the air, it now does a lengthy special stagger animation. Damaging it enough in this state will cause it to fall down. It now has its own battle theme, which is a rendition of Forest and Hills, with hints of Old Jungle. Despite it appearing in the Windward Plains in the game's reveal trailer, it does not show up there at all in the game itself. It has a turf war with Quematrice, Rompopolo, Uth Duna, and Lagiacrus. The outcome of its Turf War with Lagiacrus is dependent on whether Lagiacrus is charged up or not; Rathalos wins if it is not, and Lagiacrus wins if it is. Monster Hunter Frontier Rathalos can be a pet. HC Rathalos's roar requires HG Earplugs to block. Monster Hunter Online Rathalos was added in an update, making Monster Hunter Online the only title in the entire series that didn't have Rathalos present at launch. The Forest and Hills battle theme played for Rathalos in all locations, and was the first game to do so. Monster Hunter Now TBA Monster Hunter Stories 3 Rathalos are considered extinct in the game, similar to Arkveld in Monster Hunter Wilds. Despite so, some of the Rathalos are still alive inhabiting the Sacrosanctum, making them actually didn't go extinct. Possible that Azuria and Vermeil are clueless before Sacrosanctum expedition. Rathalos uses the New World design, and its roar is the high-pitch screech heard in Gen 5 titles. Rathalos is the first monstie given to player in this game, unlike previous entries, which have a Velocidrome as the first monstie. ▷ Monsters appearing in Monster Hunter ( edit ) Herbivore &#160; # Rathian

Rathian リオレイア &#160; (Rioreia) Carves · Equipment · Gallery · Videos Ecology English Title Female Fire Wyvern Queen of the Land Japanese Title 雌火竜 陸の女王 Chinese Name 雌火龙 (Simplified) 雌火龍 (Traditional) Chinese Title 陆之女王 (Simplified) 陸之女王 (Traditional) Korean Name 리오레이아 Korean Title 자화룡 General Information Monster Class Flying Wyvern Elements Fire Ailments Fireblight ( 3rd Gen onwards) Poison Weakest to Dragon Thunder Water ( MHXR ) Habitat Forest and Hills Old Jungle Old Desert Old Swamp Jungle Desert Swamp Great Forest Tower Tower 3 Deserted Island Sandy Plains Flooded Forest Misty Peaks Ancestral Steppe Sunken Hollow Primal Forest Heaven's Mount Everwood Tower Summit Dunes Jurassic Frontier Verdant Hills Marshlands Ruined Pinnacle Ancient Forest Wildspire Waste Guiding Lands Shrine Ruins Sandy Plains (Rise) Flooded Forest (Rise) Lava Caverns Red Stronghold Infernal Springs Jungle (MHRS) Windward Plains Scarlet Forest Oilwell Basin Great Forest Peak Gorge Highland Interceptor's Base Fortress Ruins Dawnwind Valley Dark Veil Forest Esther Lake Ancestral Tomb Moonlands Firefly Mountain Stream Monsonne Plains Size 2303.8 1151.9 Related Monsters Pink Rathian Gold Rathian Dreadqueen Rathian Apex Rathian Conflagration Rathian Scorching Heat Rathian Rathalos Azure Rathalos Silver Rathalos Dreadking Rathalos Apex Rathalos Zenith Rathalos Zerureusu Sparkling Zerureusu Flame Rathalos Destruction Wyvern Rathalos Generation First “ Fire-breathing female wyverns, also known as the "Queens of the Land". With powerful legs and poison-secreting tails, they hunt mainly on the ground. Sometimes seen preying as a couple, Rathians and Rathalos cooperate well. „ Rathian are female Flying Wyverns introduced in Monster Hunter . Contents 1 Physiology 2 Abilities 3 Behavior 4 Habitat 5 Game Appearances 5.1 Main Series 5.2 Frontier 5.3 Spin-offs 5.4 Crossovers 6 Game Data 6.1 Monster Hunter 1 6.1.1 Damage Effectiveness 6.2 Monster Hunter G 6.2.1 Damage Effectiveness 6.3 Monster Hunter 3 Breakable Parts 6.4 Monster Hunter 3 Damage Effectiveness 6.5 Monster Hunter 3 Item Effectiveness 6.6 Monster Hunter Portable 3rd Damage Effectiveness 6.7 Monster Hunter 4 Ultimate Breakable Parts 6.8 Monster Hunter World Damage Effectiveness 7 Materials 7.1 Monster Hunter 4 Ultimate 8 Equipment 9 Other Non-Subspecies Forms 9.1 Dreadqueen Rathian 9.2 Apex Rathian 9.3 Scorching Heat Rathian 10 In-Game Description 10.1 Hunting Tips 11 Gallery 12 Notes Physiology [ ] Rathian is a medium-sized true flying wyvern similar in looks to her male counterpart, Rathalos. Her hide is a dull, muted green in contrast to the Rathalos's deep red, and her lower mandible features a long, protruding chin spike. Her upper back and wingtips are covered in a moss-like fur not seen on the Rathalos, and while her clubbed tail lacks bony spikes, it makes up for this with the presence of sharp and highly poisonous needles, which she can use to poison foes upon contact. Abilities [ ] Rathian, like her mate, can generate flaming projectiles from her mouth. She is notably more proficient in the use of this weapon however, as she is commonly seen releasing multiple blasts at once. In addition, she is capable of charging up and releasing a more potent fire blast which can ignite a swath of ground in front of her. She can use the poisonous barbs on her tail club to inflict blunt-force, toxic wounds on foes, even if her tail was cut off. Her powerful legs allow her to run at high speeds, making her easily capable of chasing down most prey. Like the Rathalos, her vision is very keen, so Flash Bombs are useful in combat. Behavior [ ] Rathian is very territorial, choosing to patrol from ground while her Rathalos mate patrols from the sky. She will become very aggressive to potential threats, especially when in close proximity to her nest and young. Rathian and Rathalos will always go after hunters that are carrying their eggs. Habitat [ ] Rathians can survive in almost any territory, but prefers areas with average temperature for nesting. As a result, she is known to be encountered in a large variety of areas such as the Jungle , Sandy Plains , and Primal Forest . Game Appearances [ ] Click on Expand to see contents. Unlike its male counterpart, it usually patrols its territory from the ground. Its tail spikes are filled with poison. Monster Hunter Freedom 2 / Monster Hunter Freedom Unite Female version of the Rathalos wyvern. Unlike its male counterpart, it usually patrols its territory from the ground. Its tail spikes are filled with poison. Monster Hunter 3 Fire-breathing female wyverns, also known as "Queens of the Land." With powerful legs and poison-secreting tails, they hunt mainly on the ground. Sometimes seen preying as a couple, Rathian cooperate well with Rathalos. Monster Hunter Portable 3rd 陸の女王の異名を持つ “雌火竜” 。地上を中心とした狩りを行うため、大地を駆ける優れた脚力と、獲物を仕留める猛毒を含んだ尾を持つ。つがいで狩りをする様子も目撃されており、その際にはお互いに連携を取るかのような行動を見せるという。 Monster Hunter 3 Ultimate Fire-breathing female wyverns, also known as the "Queens of the Land". With powerful legs and poison-secreting tails, they hunt mainly on the ground. Sometimes seen preying as a couple, Rathian cooperate well with Rathalos. Threat Level (危険度): ★★★★ Monster Hunter 4 陸の女王の異名を持つ”雌火竜”。 地上を中心とした狩りを行うため、大地を駆ける優れた脚力と、獲物を仕留める猛毒を含んだ尾を持つ。 つがいで狩りをする様子も目撃されており、その際にはお互いに連携を取るかのような行動を見せるという。 Threat Level (危険度): ★★★★ Monster Hunter 4 Ultimate Fire-breathing female wyverns, also known as the "Queens of the Land". With powerful legs and poison-secreting tails, they hunt mainly on the ground. Sometimes seen preying as a couple, Rathians and Rathalos cooperate well. Threat Level (危険度): ★★★★ Monster Hunter Generations Fire-breathing female wyverns, also known as the "Queens of the Land". With powerful legs and poison-secreting tails, they hunt mainly on the ground. Sometimes seen preying as a couple, Rathians and Rathalos cooperate well. Threat Level (危険度): ★★★★ Monster Hunter World Wyverns known as the "Queens of the Land." Terrestrial predators, they overpower prey with their venomous tails and powerful legs. Monster Hunter Rise Fire-breathing female wyverns, also known as the "Queens of the Land". With powerful legs and poison-secreting tails, they hunt mainly on the ground. Sometimes seen preying as a couple, Rathians and Rathalos cooperate well. Threat Level (危険度): ★★★★ Monster Hunter Wilds Fire-breathing female wyverns known as the "Queens of the Land." Their powerful legs and venomous tails make them efficient ground hunters. At times, Rathian and Rathalos can be sighted hunting as a couple. After laying eggs, Rathian are extremely watchful of their nests. Proceed with extra caution, as a mother will move swiftly to protect her young. Monster Hunter Stories Female Flying Wyverns dubbed Queens of the Land. Possess powerful legs, and deadly, poisonous tails. Monster Hunter Stories 2: Wings of Ruin Female Flying Wyverns, dubbed Queens of the Land. Possess powerful legs and a deadly, poisonous tail. Have been sighted hunting in pairs with ruthless efficiency. Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection This Flying Wyvern earns its title of "Queen of the Land" by using its powerful legs to hunt primarily on the ground. Any challenges to her reign will be swiftly put down by the poison-laden tail. Monster Hunter Frontier G (?) Monster Hunter Online 分类 飞龙种，有着翠绿色鳞片的雌性飞龙。 生境范围 常栖息于林地与沼泽。 生态位 经常在其生息的生态系中居于顶点。因为其比起善于飞行的雄性来说更擅长奔走，主要在地面狩猎和活动，人们就对其冠以“陆之女王”的称号。 适应性 和雄性一样有着剧毒的尾刺、灼热的火焰喷吐以及瞬间致命的破坏力，覆盖全身的鳞甲能够格挡开锐利的刀剑，而最危险的猛冲突进是对挡路者意志力和勇气的终极考验。 行为 母性的本能比其他飞龙种都要强，在繁殖期间危险性比平时强上数倍。对猎人来说，雌火龙是既值得敬畏又充满吸引力的强大敌手。 Threat Level (危険度): !!!!! Hunting Tips [ ] “ Come prepared with fire-resistant armor, and neuter its poison attacks by severing its tail. Be careful when stealing an egg from its nest, for a Rathian will chase an egg thief to the ends of the New World. „ - MHW's Hunter's Notes “ The strong venom in her tail, along with the wide reach of her sweeping attacks, makes maneuvering around the Rathian a dangerous feat. Cutting off her tail can give you quite a bit more breathing room. „ - MHRise's Hunter's Notes “ The spikes that adorn this monster's tail are coated with a potent venom. However, this poison will be rendered ineffective should its tail be severed. Its head is particularly vulnerable, so it would be wise to focus your aim on it during a hunt. „ - MHNow’s Useful Information Gallery [ ] For more images, see Rathian Photo Gallery Notes [ ] General MH3 MH4U MHW MHRise MHWilds Frontier MHO General Notes Rathian actually has a higher base health than Rathalos. However, their resulting health is also calculated by the amount of stars a quest has. This means that the Rathalos will have more health in his own quests (his quests have more stars than Rathian's), but when together Rathian will have more health in the "Double Trouble" and "Mating Season" quests. When Rathian is low on stamina, her fire breath fails. However, those that are too close to her mouth can still be hurt from the flame. She also stumbles after a charge. In games prior to Monster Hunter: World , as well as in Monster Hunter Rise , Rathian's backflip attack can still poison hunters even when her tail has been severed. However, severing her tail make it easier to dodge the backflip. Occasionally, she can poison other monsters in the area with the tail flip. Rathalos can also do this with his claws. In many of the games she appears in, Rathian seems to be weaker than Rathalos. Her first quest is usually at least 1 star below Rathalos's, and if not, it has a lower contract fee and quest reward than the latter. Rathian is capable of doing an aerial roar like Rathalos, although it is very rare due to her being on the ground most of the time. Rathian's roar requires Earplugs to block. Although it has never had its own battle theme in any main series title, Rathian is mostly associated with the Old Jungle battle theme as it was designed with the wyvern in mind. The only exception of this is Monster Hunter Online , where Old Jungle plays no matter where she is fought. This holds true for Pink Rathian and Gold Rathian in the same game. Some cutscenes featuring Rathian and her subspecies, such as in Monster Hunter World , use leitmotifs of Old Jungle. Rathians are one of three monsters that have made an appearance in every game in the series. The others are Rathalos and Felyne . Rathian was voted 78th place in the Hunters' Choice for Top Monster during Monster Hunter's 20th Anniversary. &#91; 1 &#93; Rathian was the first monster to be created and designed for the first Monster Hunter game. &#91; 2 &#93; Monster Hunter 3 Rathian's design has been reworked in Monster Hunter 3. Further updates to her appearance were made in later games. Rathian's body structure seems to be thicker than Rathalos' in 3rd and 4th gen. In Third Generation games, Rathian can be called by a Qurupeco , either in the Deserted Island or Sandy Plains . Rathian feeds on small monsters like Aptonoth , Gargwa , or Slagtoth to regain stamina. When Rathian attacks her prey, she sometimes drops a shiny. Monster Hunter 4 Ultimate Beginning with Fourth Generation games, Rathian's back can be broken. Rathian has gained the ability to do aerial sweeps, similar to Rathalos. In Monster Hunter 4 and Monster Hunter 4 Ultimate , Rathian can be infected by the Frenzy Virus . Rathian no longer vocalizes after a backflip. In G Rank, Rathian can use two new attacks: An advancing side bite immediately followed by a tail spin. A running charge chained into a backflip. Monster Hunter: World Rathian's tail flips are no longer able to inflict poison if the tail is severed. Monster Hunter Rise Rathian can appear in The Rampage . It is considered a Stalker type. In Master Rank, Rathian can perform a charge before immediately going airborne to perform either a backflip or an air charge. As revealed in the final Digital Event for Monster Hunter Rise: Sunbreak , Rathian was the third most-hunted monster in Monster Hunter Rise . Monster Hunter Wilds Rathian's head and back are now unbreakable. Rathian now shares Rathalos's trait of occasionally failing to take flight after a wing is broken. It can now do Pink Rathian's advancing curved tail flip attack. Rathian's mouth can be focus struck after breathing fire to cause a stagger. Rathian's tail flips are no longer able to inflict poison if the tail is severed again after Rise reverted this change. Monster Hunter Frontier Rathian was available as a pet. Monster Hunter Online Rathian was unlocked at Level 53. The Old Jungle battle theme played for Rathian in all locations, which was the only time in the series it had a theme. ▷ Monsters appearing in Monster Hunter ( edit ) Herbivore &#160; Aptonoth &#160; # Rey Dau

Rey Dau レ・ダウ &#160; (Re Dau) Carves · Equipment · Gallery · Videos Ecology English Title Shining Thunder Wyvern Earth-Piercing Bolt Destruction's Knell ( Kunafa ) Japanese Title 煌雷竜 大地を貫く蒼き雷 Chinese Name 煌雷龙 (Simplified) 煌雷龍 (Traditional) Chinese Title 贯穿大地的苍雷 (Simplified) Korean Name 레 다우 Korean Title 황뢰룡 대지를 꿰뚫는 푸른 번개 General Information Monster Class Flying Wyvern Elements Thunder Ailments Thunderblight Weakest to Ice Water Habitat Windward Plains Bountiful Dunes Rococo Rocks Death's Maw Size 2,571.35 1,851.37 Related Monsters None Generation Sixth “ A Flying Wyvern that rules the Windward Plains as its apex predator. It has adapted to the lightning that accompanies the Sandtide, with most sightings having occurred during the storm. Its ability to discharge electricity in powerful attacks pose a threat to even the most experienced hunters. „ Rey Dau is a Flying Wyvern first introduced in Monster Hunter Wilds . Contents 1 Physiology 2 Behavior and Abilities 3 Game Appearances 3.1 Main Series 3.2 Spin-offs 4 In-Game Description 5 Notes 6 References Physiology [ ] Rey Dau is a large bipedal wyvern covered in bony golden scales, as well as smaller black scales on its underbelly, joints, and wings. Unlike most other bipedal Flying Wyverns, its wings are not comprised of extended digits, instead being a single membrane similar to that of pterosaurs . Its wings additionally have bladed protrusions which are used as weapons. It has a short and vaguely serpentine head with a facial structure similar to Nergigante , with two rows of teeth and a pair of mandible-like protrusions on either side of its jaw, while its neck has two slits that extrude glowing blue, fin-like structures whenever it is focusing electricity. Its defining features are the large horns spread out on each side of its head; the horns on its head can be folded inwards and used to channel electricity akin to a rail gun. After harnessing electricity, its wing blades and back spikes glow bright blue with fulgurite. Behavior and Abilities [ ] Rey Dau is the apex predator of the Windward Plains . It has great control over lightning, appearing during thunderstorms and using the electricity in the air to channel, charge and fire massive blasts of lightning from its horns. After blasting lightning, the sand on its wings crystallizes into sharp blades of fulgurite crystal, enhancing its attacks. Rey Dau can additionally charge its horns with electricity to stab opponents like a electric spear, as well as using acrobatic aerial attacks while slicing opponents with its bladed wings. It often nests at Wind's Gasp. Game Appearances [ ] Click on Expand to see contents. Main Series [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds (Introduced) Spin-offs [ ] Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection In-Game Description [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds The flying wyvern that reigns supreme over the Windward Plains' ecosystem. It has adapted to the Sandtide - when it most frequently appears - and is able to store, amplify, and discharge the storm's heavy lightning to lethal effect. Rey Dau is highly territorial and will attack any and all intruders on sight. The fulgurite against its carapace emits a sound that puts every monster in the region on guard. Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection A luminous gold Flying Wyvern that dwells in sandy, arid regions. Its defining features are its two horns, from which tremendous amounts of electrical energy can be discharged. Notes [ ] General MHWilds MHST3 General Notes The name "Rey Dau" comes from re (Italian for "king"), léi (雷; Chinese for "thunder"), and "ray" as in a ray of light. The "Dau" part comes from Arabic ḍawʾ (ضوء), meaning "light". &#91; 1 &#93; Before its name reveal, Rey Dau was dubbed by the Western fan community as "Thor Magala", seemingly originating from youtuber Hey Jay on Rurikhan's stream of the Summer Game Fest 2024 and discussion in the stream of how it seemed to resemble Gore Magala with its horns closed and charging. This nickname was acknowledged in a demo stream on the Monster Hunter YouTube channel. Before Arkveld's reveal, fans and developers alike thought that Rey Dau was the flagship of Wilds. Interestingly enough, all of Rey Dau's cutscenes involves it fighting Arkveld. Its horns are vulnerable to attacks after performing its railgun blasts, and focus striking them will cause a stagger. Rey Dau is the only Apex Monster to be weak to multiple elements. Rey Dau makes chime-like sounds due to fulgurite shards on its wings. For this reason, fulgurite shards are utilized by Kunafa to ward off monsters. Monster Hunter Wilds It has a turf war with Arkveld and Seregios . When its physical and thunder attacks strike an area covered in active fulgurite, thunder bursts will detonate in the places they hit. So far, this only occur in the Wind's Gasp. High Rank Rey Dau will commonly double-up their attacks. Arch-tempered Rey Dau was added in Monster Hunter Wilds' first title update, though it was not available until April 30, 2025 (UTC). It is the first non-Elder Dragon monster to be Arch-tempered. AT Rey Dau's physical attacks create thunder bursts on the ground without the need for an active fulgurite surface, while those on fulgurite have double the usual range. It also gains some new comboes, secondary thunder bursts from certain thunder attacks and a new attack where it fires a lightning bolt at the ground and then takes to the air. Monster Hunter Stories 3 It is the final boss of Ch 3 before arriving at Serathis. Its battle theme from Wilds is used for its boss fight. # Rompopolo

Rompopolo ププロポル &#160; (Pupuroporu) Carves · Equipment · Gallery · Videos Ecology English Title Swamp Spouting Wyvern Japanese Title 沼噴竜 Chinese Name 沼喷龙 (Simplified) 沼噴龍 (Traditional) Korean Name 푸푸로포루 Korean Title 소분룡 General Information Monster Class Brute Wyvern Ailments Poison Weakest to Water Habitat Oilwell Basin Wounded Hollow Size 1,497.27 1,066.05 Generation Sixth “ A brute wyvern that inhabits areas of deep oilsilt. A brute wyvern that can spray toxic gas from the tip of its tongue and inject gas into the ground with its tail, causing noxious explosions to well up from underfoot. „ Rompopolo is a Brute Wyvern first introduced in Monster Hunter Wilds . Contents 1 Physiology 2 Behavior and Abilities 3 Game Appearances 3.1 Main Series 4 In-Game Description 5 Notes Physiology [ ] Rompopolo is a brute wyvern with an unusual and bizarre appearance; it has the basic brute wyvern body plan with small arms, large hind legs and a powerful tail, but it also has a long sharp beak similar to that of a hummingbird or a mosquito proboscis, equipped with a long tongue capable of forking at the end, its short arms have long sharp front claws like a mantis, and it has gecko-like toe pads on its feet and beady, close-set reddish violet eyes that seemingly lack eyelids. Its skin has a dark blue and purple hue, with its head and the rest of its back and tail being armed with expandable sacs filled with poisonous gases, with insect-like spiracles on its sides to vent it out. Rompopolo's tail additionally has a stinger at the end capable of pumping out poisonous gases that can cause the ground around it to explode as a form of attack. Behavior and Abilities [ ] Rompopolo mainly attacks by spraying its enemies with poisonous mist from its mouth in the from of sweeping breath attacks, along with lashing at enemies with its long tongue, it can also unleash a concentrated stream of poison gas as a long ranged attack. It will slash at enemies with its front claws and use its tail as a bludgeoning weapon as well. Rompopolo can also drive its tail stinger into muddy and oily ground in order to inject it with poisonous gases, and cause the ground around it to explode and erupt with oil and tar to heavily damage opponents. Game Appearances [ ] Click on Expand to see contents. Main Series [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds (Introduced) In-Game Description [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds Dappled brute wyverns that prowl Oilwell Basin. They use their tails to inject gas into the ground and detonate it, then feed on the small creatures that are unearthed. The toxic gas they spray from their tongues serve to weaken prey, which they then finish off with the blasts. Rompopolo are extremely territorial and will readily engage their own kind. Notes [ ] General General Notes Rompopolo has a Turf War with Ajarakan , Rathian , and Rathalos . Damaging Rompopolo's sacs enough will cause them to deflate, temporarily preventing it from using its poison abilities. Deflating all the sacs at once topples it, and if enough damage is done when enraged, it will trigger a special knockdown. Breaking Rompopolo's claws causes it to fall over after using its double claw slam. It is one of the few monsters that are referred to by name by the inhabitants of the Forbidden Lands. It is one of the few Brute Wyverns, alongside Brachydios, that has well-developed front arms. The concept idea for Rompopolo is to give it the feeling of mad doctors. It was revealed in a post from Monster Hunter's X account that Rompopolo is the least hunted monster. As such, it was given a special 10★ event quest for Wilds's first anniversary, similar to the least-hunted monsters in World/Iceborne ( Lavasioth and Brute Tigrex ). This special Tempered Rompopolo's ground explosions inflict poison, it creates much more explosions with greatly increased damage, and its gas breath has significantly increased range. ▷ Monsters # Uth Duna

Uth Duna ウズ・トゥナ &#160; (Uzu Tuna) Carves · Equipment · Gallery · Videos Ecology English Title Wave Dress Wyvern Crested Torrent Japanese Title 波衣竜 羽衣を纏いし奔流 Chinese Name 波衣龙 (Simplified) 波衣龍 (Traditional) Chinese Title 缠绕羽衣的奔流 (Simplified) Korean Name 우드 투나 Korean Title 파의룡 날개옷을 걸친 격류 General Information Monster Class Leviathan Elements Water Ailments Waterblight Weakest to Thunder Habitat Scarlet Forest Size 3,426.18 2,681.36 Generation Sixth “ A Leviathan that rules the Scarlet Forest as its apex predator. It has adapted to water-rich environments and is sighted most often during the Downpour. It makes use of the moisture and its own bodily fluids to create a protective veil around itself. „ Uth Duna is a Leviathan first introduced in Monster Hunter Wilds . Contents 1 Physiology 2 Behavior and Abilities 3 Game Appearances 3.1 Main Series 4 In-Game Description 5 Notes Physiology [ ] Uth Duna is a massive reptilian Leviathan with a hefty quadrupedal body. It has a narrow, arapaima fish-like face equipped with powerful jaws and small sharp teeth. Its body is covered in colorful red and lustrous scales in a striped pattern that looks similar to the ones of an Arapaima. Uth Duna's most recognizable features are the massive translucent and watery "fins" on its head, back, forelimbs, and tail formed from a mix of water and mucous secretions along several long tentacles on the leviathans head, limbs, and back, and on these tentacles are lined with long cilia which act as the skeletal structure of Uth Duna's watery fins. When these fins are devoid of water and damaged, Uth Duna will retract these tentacles and its body appears more noticeably seal-like; visibly heavy, yet streamlined and graceful in water. Behavior and Abilities [ ] Uth Duna is the apex predator of the Scarlet Forest . It mainly appears during the downpour where the river water levels are higher, giving it an advantage in its watery habitat. Despite its bulky appearance, Uth Duna possesses immense speed and grace when in the water, quickly leaping out of the water and crashing back down to crush its opponents. As well as biting with its large, toothy jaws, it will also swipe at hunters with its large, clawed arms. Its powerful body gives it the ability to create huge splashes and waves to knock hunters off their feet. It can use the surrounding moisture and its body fluids to create a protective veil of water as a defense mechanism, resulting in its watery veiled fins and tail. These water bound appendages provide massive damage reduction from hunter attacks, by breaking these watery fins Uth Duna will become more vulnerable to attack; Conversely, the veil weighs the Leviathan down and with its reduced protection, its attacks become faster and more aggressive. Game Appearances [ ] Click on Expand to see contents. Main Series [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds (Introduced) In-Game Description [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds The leviathan that reigns supreme over the Scarlet Forest's ecosystem. It has adapted to water-rich environments and is sighted most often during the Downpour. Uth Duna's secretions when combined with water form a protective veil that not only absorbs hostile blows but also adds weight, which it slams down with enough force to sweep away surrounding threats in the resulting wave. Notes [ ] General General Notes The name Uth Duna is a transliteration of the Japanese name, which may or may not derive from uzu (うず), Japanese for "whirlpool", as well as Tuna , a god of eels. Uth Duna has unique protrusible jaws like a lot of fish species possess. It has a turf war with Rathalos, Mizutsune, and Lagiacrus. Rathalos will poison Uth Duna in their turf war. Uth Duna can be power clashed by blocking its head swings, bites, or claw strikes. Arch-tempered Uth Duna was added in Monster Hunter Wilds' second title update, though it was not available until July 23, 2025 (UTC). AT Uth Duna gains three new attacks; a charging rush that pivots in a U-turn, a double claw swipe, and a sideways leap and roll. Its leaping attacks are performed significantly faster. ▷ Monsters appearing in Monster Hunter Wilds ( edit ) Herbivore &#160; Ceratonoth &#160; Dalthydon &#160; Rafma Neopteron &#160; Bulaqchi &#160; # Xu Wu

Xu Wu シーウー &#160; (Shīū) Carves · Equipment · Gallery · Videos Ecology English Title Concealed Weapon Octopus Piercing Assassin Japanese Title 暗器蛸 Chinese Name 暗器蛸 (Simplified) 暗器鱆 (Traditional) Korean Name 시이우 Korean Title 암기소 General Information Monster Class Cephalopod Weakest to Ice Habitat Ruins of Wyveria Wounded Hollow Size 1,745.37 1,242.70 Related Monsters Nu Udra Generation Sixth Xu Wu is a Cephalopod first introduced in Monster Hunter Wilds . Contents 1 Physiology 2 Behavior and Abilities 3 Game Appearances 3.1 Main Series 4 In-Game Description 5 Notes Physiology [ ] Xu Wu is an eight-limbed cephalopod with a body structure similar to an octopus. Six of its limbs are long and flexible, located on the sides of its body, while another pair, heavily reduced, is located in front of its body. Its six limbs have golden, sharp spikes that acts as blades and spears. Its skin is a greenish-black color with a gnarled texture, and several gold and teal veins running through it. The underside of its body glows bright teal, and is lined with sharp spikes. Above them is a complex of root-like appendages, as well as a patch of hard plating. Atop Xu Wu's head is a crown that greatly resembles a mouth, although, the monster's real mouth is located at the underside of its body, glowing blue. Its head is similar in shape to mushrooms, and in some sort, aliens. It's dark color scheme and appearance also are somewhat reminiscent of a ninja. Newly hatched Xu Wu are known as Xu Yo. They are spherical in shape, with undeveloped tentacles, no visible eyes or mouth and a glowing greenish-yellow body. Behavior and Abilities [ ] Xu Wu secretes golden mucus from its limbs that harden into razor-sharp blades. It can swipe these blades and stab at enemies or fling them as projectiles. Xu Wu can also extend its tentacles to a great degree, increasing its attack range greatly. It is remarkably agile and can reposition itself quickly before launching an attack. Its strongest attack has it rear up with its underside and launch itself at enemies for tremendous damage; this can be interrupted to topple it. Occasionally, Xu Wu will surround itself in a thick white mist to distract its foe before flattening its body and quickly repositioning itself around its prey; this mist will leak from the Cephalopods' head when enraged. Xu Wu is known to feed on Construct monsters, striking quickly with the bladed tentacle before trying to swallow it. Thus, its position at the top of the food chain in Wyveria makes it the ecological "apex" of the area. Game Appearances [ ] Click on Expand to see contents. Main Series [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds (Introduced) In-Game Description [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds Cephalopod predators whose prey includes Guardian monsters. The mucus secreted from their tentacles hardens quickly into sharp edges that resemble blades or spears, which is why some call Xu Wu the "Piercing Assassin." The sizable mouth hidden on their underside allows them to devour even rather large prey. Notes [ ] General General Notes Xu Wu's head, mouth, and front limbs can be broken and its back limbs can be severed. Xu Wu, like Arkveld , has the tendency to knock over Guardian Doshaguma , Guardian Fulgur Anjanath and Guardian Rathalos before the prompt "Turf War" appears, seemingly trying to devour them; Guardian Ebony Odogaron is the only monster to have proper turf war with Xu Wu. But then again, there's a chance that Guardian Ebony Odogaron will get knockdown first before the prompt "Turf War" appears like the rest of the Guardians. Xu Wu's name is a reference to the Chinese word for "nothingness" 虚无 ( Xūwú ). This is further highlighted by the name of its assignment and its optional quest, which both reference the void; and by its weapons' names, which all start with "Nihil," the Latin word for "nothing." The most upgraded versions of its weapons are named after famous historical traitors, assasins and usurpers, with their names slightly corrupted. Xu Wu has a pin attack where it grabs the hunter and chews them up if not escaped. If the attack’s damage would cause a faint, Xu Wu will swallow the hunter as well. ▷ Monsters appearing in Monster Hunter Wilds ( edit ) Herbivore &#160; Ceratonoth &#160; Dalthydon &#160; Rafma # Yian Kut-Ku

Yian Kut-Ku イャンクック &#160; (I~yankukku) Carves · Equipment · Gallery · Videos Ecology English Title Strange Bird Japanese Title 怪鳥 Chinese Name 大怪鸟 (Simplified) / 大怪鳥 (Traditional) 怪鸟 (Simplified) / 怪鳥 (Traditional) Korean Name 얀쿡크 Korean Title 괴조 General Information Monster Class Bird Wyvern Elements Fire Ailments Fireblight Weakest to Water on Wings Ice on Head Habitat Forest and Hills Old Jungle Old Volcano Jungle Swamp Ancestral Steppe Everwood Jurassic Frontier Verdant Hills Marshlands Ruined Pinnacle Scarlet Forest Iceshard Cliffs (Frenzied Only) Wounded Hollow Tide Island Fortress Ruins Hermit Forest Esther Lake Pondry Hills Hakolo Island Sunpetal Plains Unexplored Cavern Size 1233.23 449.3 Related Monsters Blue Yian Kut-Ku Yian Garuga Scarred Yian Garuga Deadeye Yian Garuga One-Eared Yian Garuga Generation First “ A bird wyvern with a huge beak and large ears that splay open when it is angered. „ - MHGU Description Yian Kut-Ku is a Bird Wyvern first introduced in Monster Hunter . Contents 1 Physiology 2 Behavior and Abilities 3 Habitat 4 Game Appearances 4.1 Main Series 4.2 Frontier 4.3 Spin-offs 4.4 Crossovers 5 Game Data 5.1 Physiology 5.2 Breakable Parts 5.3 Special Damage Charts 6 Materials 7 Equipment 7.1 Armor 7.2 Weapons 8 In-Game Description 9 Other Variants 9.1 Hard Core (HC) Yian Kut-Ku 9.2 Yian Kut-Rex 10 Notes Physiology [ ] The Yian Kut-Ku is characterized by its large, fan-like ear frills that unfurl when excited and enlarged lower bill. They have a large, yellow beak with a pink stripe running central down the length of the upper beak. The lower jaw of its beak is significantly larger and bulkier than the top jaw. Yian Kut-Ku's body is covered in orange-pink plating and beige scales. Its wing membranes are deep blue and its eyes are bright yellow. These wyverns are small and relatively light, making them quick and agile. They have an extremely powerful sense of hearing, thanks to the large frills circling their ears. Behavior and Abilities [ ] The Yian Kut-Ku is capable of moving at high speeds thanks to its light weight and small stature. It is also able to produce flaming projectiles coming from its flame sac, which can be shot from the mouth at attackers. It can spew three flaming projectiles at hunters when enraged and move faster. A somewhat temperamental, skittish, and inquisitive wyvern. Yian Kut-Ku have a tendency to want to appear bigger than they really are by spreading their wings, flaring out their ears, and squawking loudly. They are generally insectivores, preying on Konchu and other Neopterons as a main part of their diet. In the Scarlet Forest, they are shown to live in packs. Habitat [ ] Yian Kut-Ku can be seen inhabiting a wide variety of locations such as the Verdant Hills and the Old Volcano . Game Appearances [ ] Click on Expand to see contents. Value: 2250z Rare-8 Kut-Ku Cortex 怪鳥の重殻 A stout outer shell, much harder than a carapace. Also called Mediastinum. Value: 3105z Rare-8 Kut-Ku Weave 怪鳥の厚翼膜 Bulky webbing from a Kut-Ku wing, valued for its greater than normal powers of endurance. Value: 5850z Rare-8 Kut-Ku Luckear 怪鳥の福耳 A full grown Kut-Ku ear. Considered a good luck charm due to its texture and form. Value: 5120z Hyper Rare-8 Hyper Kut-Ku Shard 獰猛化怪鳥厚鱗 Darker than normal Kut-Ku scales, this tough Hyper scale emits an ominous glow. Value: 2700z Equipment [ ] For more information, see Yian Kut-Ku Equipment Armor [ ] Yian Kut-Ku materials can be used to craft the following armor in Monster Hunter 4 Ultimate : Kut-Ku Armor (Blademaster) Kut-Ku Armor (Gunner) Kut-Ku S Armor (Blademaster) Kut-Ku S Armor (Gunner) Kut-Ku X Armor (Blademaster) Kut-Ku X Armor (Gunner) Weapons [ ] The following is a list of Yian Kut-Ku weapons in Monster Hunter Generations Ultimate : Great Sword : Chicken Decapitator Rooster Decapitator Capon Decapitator Dual Blades : Kut-Ku Pair Dual Kut-Ku Dual Kut-Ku Kut-Ku Hammer: Kut-Ku Pick Kut-Ku Thwap Kut-Ku Kaboom Hunting Horn: Kut-Ku's Song Kut-Ku Cantabile Kut-Ku Tempestoso Switch Axe: Beak Axe Sawbill Axe Terror Bird Axe Insect Glaive: Kut-Ku Pike Toucan Pike Flamebeak Pike Light Bowgun: Kut-Ku Anger Kut-Ku Counterattack Kut-Ku Rampage Heavy Bowgun: Yian Kut-Ku Cannon Super Kut-Ku Cannon Gigantic Kut-Ku Cannon Bow: Kut-Ku Stave Strange Kut-Ku Stave Great Kut-Ku Stave In-Game Description [ ] Monster Hunter Freedom A bird-like wyvern with a huge beak and large ears that splay open when it is angered. With its sensitive hearing it dislikes loud noises. It is smaller but faster than other wyverns. Monster Hunter Freedom 2 / Monster Hunter Freedom Unite A bird-like wyvern with a huge beak and large ears that splay open when it is angered. With its sensitive hearing it dislikes loud noises. It is smaller but faster than other wyverns. Monster Hunter 4 Ultimate A bird wyvern with a huge beak and large ears that splay open when it is angered. Due to its sensitive hearing it dislikes loud noises. It is smaller but faster than other wyverns. Threat Level (危険度): ★★★ Monster Hunter Generations Ultimate A bird wyvern with a huge beak and large ears that splay open when it is angered. Quick on its feet, and known to be territorial. It's known for its love of Konchu, and has been observed digging them up out of the ground to swallow them whole. Threat Level (危険度): ★★★ Monster Hunter Wilds Bird wyverns with impressive beaks and large ears. They tend to be skittish but will sometimes initiate attacks on a perceived threat. Yian Kut-Ku arrive in large groups during the season of Plenty in the Scarlet Forest. The absence of eggs or young suggests that the purpose of this is not to breed, but rather to seek out mates and nourishment. Monster Hunter Stories Monsters boasting ample beaks and fan-like ears that splay open when angered. Konchu are a staple food. Monster Hunter Stories 2: Wings of Ruin These monsters boast ample beaks and fan-like ears that splay open when angered. A cowardly species, they have dexterous, nimble legs perfect for running away. However, they show little mercy to smaller creatures that encroach on their territory. Konchu are a staple food for Yian Kut-Ku. Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection A Bird Wyvern commonly found in warm regions, it has a distinctive large beak and wide ears that splay out to intimidate potential threats. It is docile by nature and flees more than it fights. Monster Hunter Online 分类 鸟龙种。 生境范围 杂食性，活动范围很广并且繁殖能力极强。 生态位 被猎人们戏称为“教官”，得到这种称呼主要是因为其数量足够多以外，相对其他更加凶暴的怪物来说危险性也较低的关系。 适应性 大怪鸟自身也是一种较为胆小的怪物，不过因为其非常容易紧张，往往会神经质地主动袭击无害的对手。有些学者认为这是因为大怪鸟那招牌式的巨大耳廓会把周围的声音数十倍地放大，因此哪怕没有敌意的举动也会引起大怪鸟的激烈反应。 行为 大怪鸟的攻击以猛冲和火球喷吐为主，其身体正前方区域都处于他的攻击范围之中。远距离时，他的快速突进，近身时，他的啄击都会对猎人造成不小的威胁。 Threat Level (危険度): !! Other Variants [ ] Hard Core (HC) Yian Kut-Ku [ ] Hardcore Yian Kut-Ku is much more pink than a standard Yian Kut-Ku. Hardcore Yian Kut-Ku can perform a circular rampaging charge attack with extreme attack power, and has the ability to spew a Fire Ball that causes a wall of fire. Yian Kut-Rex [ ] Monster Hunter Online introduced an April Fools quest with a Yian Kut-Ku as the target. This Yian Kut-Ku shows no odd signs initially, but upon entering Abnormal State it assumes the skeleton, animations, moveset and theme of Tigrex . It will exactly fight and act like a Tigrex, but instead of throwing rocks it throws fireballs. Notes [ ] General MHFU MH4U MHGenU MHWilds Frontier MHO MHST2 MHST3 General Notes The name "Kut-Ku" may come from the word "cuckoo" for its bird-like characteristic and appearance. The large frills or discs around its ears function similarly to the facial discs of owls, enhancing their hearing. Yian Kut-Ku was voted 35th place in the Hunters' Choice for Top Monster during Monster Hunter's 20th Anniversary. &#91; 1 &#93; Monster Hunter Freedom Unite There is a quest called "At Birds Hill..." which requires players to fight an extremely small Yian Kut-Ku at the Forest and Hills zone, which is most-likely a young Yian Kut-Ku. It has a different voice due to its miniscule size, yet its fireball attack increases in power and explosion radius, this may be because it doesn't have very good control over its Flame Sac. Yian Kut-Ku can be sometimes be seen drinking from the ponds in Areas 9 and 10 of the Forest and Hills. Monster Hunter 4 Ultimate The Yian Kut-Ku has 4 toes while in previous generations, it had 3 toes. Yian Kut-Ku will prey on Konchu &#160;to regain stamina. Yian Kut-Ku is now capable to release 2 fireballs instead of 1. While low on stamina, Yian Kut-Ku won't be able to shoot fireballs, and its beak will get stuck in the ground when it performs its pecking attack. In G-Rank, Yian Kut-Ku can dig up Konchu and throw them at the hunter. Monster Hunter Generations Ultimate G-Rank Yian Kut-Ku will only use its Konchu throw attack in maps where Konchu naturally spawn. Monster Hunter Wilds The Yian Kut-Ku's wings have the redesign given to the Yian Garuga in Iceborne. Yian Kut-Ku now form and can fight in groups during the Scarlet Forest 's Plenty season. They are the largest pack in the game, with six individuals able to appear at once. When the pack is present, several non-interactable Kut-Ku will appear in the background of their nest. Yian Kut-Ku appears in the Iceshard Cliffs, despite never appearing in tundras; however, only the Frenzied kind appears in the locale. They gain the beak slam from their Blue Yian Kut-Ku counterparts, a singular fire spit even larger than the quadruple version and a rush having it dig its jaw through the ground with high tracking, similar to Deadeye Yian Garuga. Normally weaker than Nerscylla, a Frenzied kind was able to kill and tear off a leg of a Frenzied Nerscylla in Gore Magala's introduction scene. Monster Hunter Frontier Yian Kut-Ku can be a pet, its favorite foods are insects and worms. Monster Hunter Online This monster is unlocked at Level 10. Yian Kut-Ku's ears can be broken twice. Yian Kut-Ku's ears will fold back when affected by a Sonic Bomb and are not used as a weakness sign. Monster Hunter Stories 2 Yian Kut-Ku is a Technical type monster. Opposing Yian Kut-Kus turn to Speed attacks when enraged. Breaking its head with a pierce type weapon (Gunlance, Bow) will boost its retreat rate. Monster Hunter Stories 3 It uses its Wilds design, but retains the use of its classic sound design. Its Kinship Attack is no longer comical; instead, it runs and eats 3 Konchus and then hurls its head back and then blasts them. ▷ Monsters appearing in Monster Hunter ( edit ) Herbivore &#160; # Zoh Shia

Zoh Shia ゾ・シア &#160; (Zo Shia) Carves · Equipment · Gallery · Videos Ecology English Name Zoh Shia English Title White Seraph Dragon Japanese Title 白熾龍 Chinese Name 白炽龙 (Simplified) 白熾龍 (Traditional) Korean Name 조 시아 Korean Title 백열룡 General Information Monster Class Construct Elements Fire Thunder Ailments Fireblight Thunderblight Weakest to Dragon Habitat Dragontorch Shrine Size 4,623.59 Generation Sixth Zoh Shia is a Construct monster first introduced in Monster Hunter Wilds , and the final boss of said game's Low-Rank. Contents 1 Physiology 2 Behavior and Abilities 3 Game Appearances 3.1 Main Series 4 In-Game Description 5 Notes Physiology [ ] Zoh Shia's initial appearance takes the form of an "angelic" dragon superficially resembling that of a six-limbed Elder Dragon . Its body covered in a sleek, paste-white shell of pure Wylk ("Wyvern Milk") crystal with a smooth, featureless face and numerous crystal formations all over its body and wing arms that curve upwards like feathers. Its mouth, which is normally not visible when closed, has unnatural blue flesh and small teeth. The Wylk crystals on its limbs give all its claws a feathery covering. Aside from the Wylk coating, the flesh itself is smooth and black with glowing veins of blue light similar to other Constructs . As more of the Construct's crystal shell is destroyed faster than it can regenerate, Zoh Shia's body mutates drastically into a horrific coal-black abomination. The head and jaw grow longer with grotesque teeth filling its' maw, and numerous twisted horns sprout from its face and arms like malignant tumors. Despite Zoh Shia's apparent eyeless-ness, when it's initial form's Wylk helmet is broken, a single pitch-black eyeball can be seen on the side of the head until the crystal regenerates. Shockingly, after the second form's head is broken, the eye that emerges from underneath the mass of broken horns is now pointing slightly upwards, and resembles the eyes of Fatalis . Behavior and Abilities [ ] Zoh Shia was the ultimate secret weapon of the Ancient Civilization of Wyveria , created to defend the empire against an unknown threat. However, the Elder Dragon -like Construct instead turned out to be too powerful to control and ended up causing the fall of the Ancient Civilization thousands of years ago. The few survivors of the fall had managed to seal Zoh Shia away in the Dragontorch Shrine where it then began to feed on the limitless energy of the Dragontorch (the source of Wylk) that Wyveria once used to manipulate natural phenomena for their own benefit during their golden age. This caused said phenomena (now known as "Inclemencies") to occur regularly throughout the Forbidden Lands , with many species eventually adapting to them. At first, it attacks with a combination of physical attacks and bursts of Wylk explosions, like other Guardian monsters only on a grander scale. Also like the Guardians, Zoh Shia can regenerate damaged parts of its crystal shell when wounded. Following it's mutation, it becomes much more adept at Fire and Thunder attacks, its abilities rivalling those of all three Fatalis species (it can use the Inferno and charged fireballs of Iceborne's Fatalis , the leaping flames and meteors of Crimson Fatalis , and the red lightning of White Fatalis ). It is thought to be able to replicate itself through a means similar to asexual reproduction, meaning even after its death it can return; however it must be in close proximity to a massive source of energy, such as the Dragontorch, to be able to do this. Despite this limitation, it can do so if even a microscopic piece of its body remains intact, making it effectively indestructible. Game Appearances [ ] Click on Expand to see contents. Main Series [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds (Introduced) In-Game Description [ ] Monster Hunter Wilds A massive Guardian that has been feeding off the Dragontorch's energy. Its body is encased in crystallized Wyvern Milk, allowing it to regenerate. Zoh Shia, like other Guardians, excels at energy attacks and can produce repeated explosions to crystallize the whole area. Beneath its white Guardian coat, its body pulses with dark recollection - which you may see, should it reveal its full power from the days of yore. Notes [ ] General General Notes The concept of Zoh Shia seems to take inspiration from the Equal Dragon Weapon of the Hunter's Encyclopedia 1 . This monster is the final boss of Monster Hunter Wilds 's Low-Rank story. Zoh Shia's face (below the Wylk helm) appears to have some effect that alters and blurs it, contributing to it's "unnatural" feel. Prior to Title Update 1, Zoh Shia could be fought only once. Once defeated, it could not be encountered again. As of Title Update 1, High Rank Zoh Shia appears as target of an Assigned Quest unlocked after reaching HR 50 and slaying Arkveld , and can randomly appear in the Dragontorch Shrine after the quest is complete. After reaching HR 100, players can access an Event Quest to slay Zoh Shia, although it seems to be markedly more difficult than the "standard" random appearance. Zoh Shia's investigations will always have the maximum five star threat level. The one-time only Low Rank Zoh Shia lacks any unique materials and equipment. Only with the High rank version can special carves be obtained and its own equipment crafted. Zoh Shia can be carved 6 times and its tail can be carved twice. Zoh Shia shares attacks with several of the most powerful and infamous Elder Dragons in the series, including: Fatalis , Crimson Fatalis , White Fatalis , Alatreon , Shara Ishvalda , Safi'jiiva , and Gaismagorm . It's possible that Zoh Shia was created to combat these Elder Dragons. Zoh Shia is, in Japanese, referred to as a "forbidden-class" (禁忌級) monster, having lead some fans to believe it to be a Forbidden Monster , but the context and meaning here is likely different. Prior to Gogmazios 's introduction in Title Update 4, it was the largest monster fought in Monster Hunter Wilds . Zoh Shia's map icon bears some resemblance to Fire Emblem Fates's Revelation icon. ▷ Monsters appearing in Monster Hunter Wilds ( edit ) Herbivore &#160; Ceratonoth &#160; Dalthydon &#160; Rafma
